Title: Police Seize Pistol After Youth Gang Shooting Kills Teenager in Pathum Thani
Location: Bridge over Klong 11, Rangsit–Nakhon Nayok Road, Bueng Nam Rak Subdistrict, Thanyaburi District, Pathum Thani
Description: Members of the Ban Love Gang opened fire across a bridge over Klong 11 using 9mm and 11mm pistols, killing a 17-year-old (Yotphon, alias Pon) from Dusit District, Bangkok, and wounding two companions on 26 March 2026. The visiting group of around 15 youths from Dusit had traveled to the Klong 11 area to confront the Ban Love Gang over an earlier assault on a mutual acquaintance's girlfriend; after leaving the gang's neighborhood, attackers riding three or four motorcycles and a black sedan opened fire from the opposite side of the bridge, striking Yotphon who fell from his motorcycle and was then shot again at close range. Casings from 9mm and 11mm ammunition were recovered at the scene, and CCTV captured the attack clearly. On 10 April 2026, police arrested three Ban Love Gang members — two juveniles aged 17 and one adult (Wutthikrai, 38) — at a rented room in Tha Bon Subdistrict, Khanu Worlakburi District, Kamphaeng Phet, seizing a 9mm CZ75B pistol.
Actors:
Youth Gang (Ban Love Gang, visiting Dusit youth group)
Police (Thanyaburi Police Station, Pathum Thani PPO)
Tags: Seizure, Crime, Gang violence, Interpersonal dispute

Actors:

Title: Gunmen Used Weapons Linked to Numerous Security Incidents in Narathiwat Assassination
Location: Chanae District, Narathiwat
Description:
Gunmen ambushed and fatally shot the Deputy District Chief of Chanae District and a female companion in their vehicle in Chanae District, Narathiwat on 1 April 2026, in an attack consistent with recurring insurgent violence against civilian officials in Thailand's southern border provinces. The attackers fired on the couple as they drove, then fled the scene; both victims died of their wounds.

Ballistic analysis revealed that four weapons were used in the attack: one AK-pattern assault rifle linked to four prior violent incidents in the area since March 2019; one M16 assault rifle linked to seven prior incidents since March 2023; one .45 caliber pistol linked to four cases since February 2009; and one 9mm pistol linked to four incidents since October 2021.
Weapons:
1 AK-pattern assault rifle
1 M16 assault rifle
1 .45 caliber pistol
1 9mm pistol
94 rounds of ammunition (referenced in early reporting)
Actors:
NSAG (presumed BRN or affiliated)
Civilian Official (Deputy District Chief of Chanae)

Title: Cyber Police Arrest Two for Manufacturing and Selling Gun Barrels Online in Phitsanulok
Location: Tambon Matong, Amphoe Phrom Phiram, Phitsanulok
Description:
Cyber Crime Investigation Bureau officers seized 10 gun barrels, one modified blank-firing handgun, and 25 rounds of ammunition when they arrested two suspects for manufacturing and selling illegal gun barrels through an online platform at a machine shop in Tambon Matong, Phrom Phiram District, Phitsanulok on 1 April 2026. Police also seized manufacturing equipment and raw materials (steel rods). The suspects were producing barrels on commission at approximately 150 baht per piece and selling them to buyers via online channels.

Following monitoring of illicit firearm sales online, police initiated a buy-bust operation in which they purchased one package with four barrels. One of the suspects admitted to selling each finished barrel for THB 1,450 and paying the lathe operator who machined the barrels for THB 150 each. The machine operator claimed he was unaware that his actions were illegal.
Weapons:
10 gun barrels
1 modified blank-firing handgun
25 rounds of ammunition
Actors:
Criminal Individual(s) (2 suspects — illegal gun barrel manufacturer and seller)
Police (Cyber Crime Investigation Bureau, CCIB)
Tags: Illicit production, Illicit transfer, Online, Conversion, Seizure

Title: Police Seize Revolver from Online Gun Dealer in Buy-Bust in Bangkok

A room search also uncovered 2.03 grams of crystal methamphetamine, 40 methamphetamine tablets, and drug paraphernalia. Panya confessed that the revolver had been left as collateral by a third party and subsequently forfeited; he had listed it for sale online to raise cash. He was transferred to Pracha Chuen Police Station for prosecution.

Officers raided an address in Moo 5, Thung Kong Subdistrict and arrested Sakda Thepchu, 58, seizing 1 pistol, 1 craft air rifle (pellet type), 3 air rifles (dome-head pellets), 1 rifle barrel, and 43 rounds of ammunition. Investigation revealed both men had purchased illegal firearm accessories through a well-known online marketplace from a seller previously arrested in Nakhon Sawan. Sakda was charged with illegal possession of firearms; Thanadech faced the additional charge of possessing prohibited war materiel (the suppressor tube) under the Arms Control Act B.E. 2530.

Title: Police and Military Discover Grenade and Ammunition Cache in Phayao
Location: Ban Kha Bon, Pa Sang Subdistrict, Dok Kham Tai District, Phayao
Description: Police and military explosive ordnance disposal (EOD) teams seized 16 grenades and 154 rounds of ammunition from a private house suspected of receiving diverted military ordnance in Ban Kha Bon, Pa Sang Subdistrict, Dok Kham Tai District, Phayao on 3 April 2026. Officers from the Phayao PPO Investigative Unit, acting on information that the location was a receiving point for weapons sourced from a military base, raided the house together with soldiers from Infantry Regiment 17, Battalion 4, Khun Jom Tham Camp. The cache comprised fragmentation grenades of three types (3× M33, 2× M26, 3× M49 munitions [presumed trip-flare mines]), 5× MK3A2 concussion grenades, 3 smoke grenades, 3 tear gas canisters, 150 rounds of 5.56mm ammunition, 2 rounds of 7.62mm, and 2 rounds of 6.35mm. The EOD team secured all items before they were handed to investigators. Authorities were pursuing the chain of supply and any broader trafficking network.
Weapons:
3 M33 (M67) hand grenades
2 M26 hand grenades
3 M49 munitions (presumed trip-flare mines)
5 MK3A2 concussion grenades
3 smoke grenades
3 tear gas canisters
154 rounds of ammunition
Actors:
Criminal Organization (military weapons diversion network)
Police (Phayao PPO Investigative Unit)
Military (Infantry Regiment 17, Battalion 4 — EOD)

Title: Police Seize Shotgun Following Deadly Youth Gang Confrontation in Bangkok
Location: Soi Ekachai 110, Bang Bon Nuea Subdistrict, Bang Bon District, Bangkok
Description: Police seized a 12-gauge craft handgun when they arrested two youth gang members following a deadly confrontation at Soi Ekachai 110 in Bang Bon, Bangkok on 5 April 2026. The confrontation left one dead and two injured. The incident was triggered at a nearby birthday party when a member of one group refused to drink with the opposing group, leading to an argument; both sides then arranged a confrontation via social media. When the Phothichaeo group arrived on motorcycles at Soi Ekachai 110, the opposing group opened fire with a 12-gauge shotgun, striking three people: Suphachok Phan-phueak, 21, was shot twice in the back and later died; a 16-year-old male was shot once in the back; and a third man, Kritsada Aphirak, 35, also sustained injuries. Police arrested the shooter (alias "Bik") and his accomplice (Nat, 35) at Nat's house inside the soi and recovered the shotgun hidden on the balcony. The shooter stated he had bought the weapon from an incarcerated friend for THB 4,000.
Actors:
Youth Gang (shooter "Bik" and accomplice Nat, 35; affiliated with Soi Ekachai 110 group)
Police (Bang Bon Police Station)
Tags: Seizure, Crime, Gang violence, Interpersonal dispute, Online, Illicit transfer

Title: Gang Members Use Craft Shotguns in Deadly Shooting at Military Conscription Event in Sisaket
Location: Khukhan District Office, Khukhan District, Sisaket
Description: Two brothers using homemade 12-gauge shotguns fatally shot Nawakorn Khampong (21) and wounded Piphat Phongsuwon (15) outside Khukhan District Office during the military conscription lottery in Sisaket on 5 April 2026. The attackers — Aphichon Wongphinit ("Bik", 22) and his brother Sarawut Wongphinit ("Non", 21) — arrived on two motorcycles, parked near a market, crossed the street to the district office and opened fire, then fled. CCTV captured both the approach and the escape. Nawakorn was shot in the chest and died at Khukhan Hospital; Piphat was struck in the left heel and survived. Investigators identified and arrested both brothers the following day; they confessed to using one homemade shotgun each and claimed self-defense, alleging that a 30-person group affiliated with the victims had attacked them first. Both tested positive for narcotics.
Actors:
Youth Gang (Aphichon Wongphinit, 22 and Sarawut Wongphinit, 21 — brothers; victim group linked to Nawakorn Khampong)
Police (Khukhan Police Station, Sisaket PPO)

Location: Moo 8, Tha Mai Ruak Subdistrict, Tha Yang District, Phetchaburi
Description: Police seized 1 M16 Colt assault rifle and 40 rounds of ammunition abandoned by a fleeing arms broker who was subsequently arrested in Tha Mai Ruak Subdistrict, Tha Yang District, Phetchaburi on 5 April 2026. Title: Police Seize Revolver After Salesman Shoots Coworker in Phuket Warehouse
Location: Ratsada Subdistrict, Mueang Phuket District, Phuket
Description: Police seized 1 revolver and 25 rounds of ammunition from Prakit Phanthanurai ("Kok"), 50, after he shot his coworker Wirat Nawadech ("Bao"), 40, twice inside the storage warehouse of a retail company in Ratsada Subdistrict, Phuket on 6 April 2026. Prakit and Wirat were both sales staff at the same store; Prakit shot Wirat once in the torso and once in the thigh after a prolonged series of workplace arguments, then fled on a motorcycle. CCTV footage captured the attack. Wirat survived — his employee ID card had partially absorbed the force of one round — and underwent surgery. The following day (7 April), Phuket City Police and Region 8 CID investigators traced Prakit to a daily-rate hotel in Talat Yai Subdistrict, Mueang Phuket, where they arrested him. He confessed and was charged with attempted murder, illegal possession and carrying of a firearm, and firing a weapon in a public/community area.

Location: Ban Na Khe Tha, Moo 3, Na Khe Subdistrict, Ban Phaeng District, Nakhon Phanom

Title: Police Seize Nine Firearms and Drugs from Machinist in Samut Sakhon
Location: Moo 5, Don Kai Dee Subdistrict, Krathum Baen District, Samut Sakhon
Description:
Krathum Baen Police seized 9 firearms, 2 suppressors, 2 bulletproof vests, hundreds of rounds of ammunition, and methamphetamine tablets from Anat ("Tam"), 45, a machinist workshop owner, on 23 April 2026, following a shooting incident. That morning, Ka Lay To, 22 (Myanmar national), was struck by a stray bullet in the leg while waiting outside a factory complex for the start of his shift; witnesses said the sound came from Anat's adjacent workshop. Officers summoned Anat, who initially refused to emerge; when he finally came out he was belligerent and resisted briefly before being restrained.

A search of the two-storey commercial warehouse found 9 loaded firearms distributed throughout. Anat also possessed several suppressor tubes and had modified some firearms for automatic fire. He told investigators he bought the weapons from friends and online over many years for self-protection, as he felt unsafe with many foreign workers nearby. He was charged with illegal possession of firearms, illegal possession of prohibited war materiel (suppressors/bulletproof vests/automatic-fire modifications), and drug possession; his firearms licenses were ordered revoked.

Description: Ob Luang National Park officials seized 3 craft rifles and arrested 3 poachers found cooking wildlife at a forest campsite in Huai Mae Kik Luang, Moo 1, Kong Khaek Subdistrict, Mae Chaem District, Chiang Mai on 23 April 2026. Officers had observed flashlights in the forest the preceding night (22 April) during a wildfire patrol and lay in wait until the following morning, when they located the group at a riverside camp. The three men — Suradej Kawichai (45), Phirun Kham-fu (31), and Kiatikrai Thip-kham (39) — admitted they had camped in the area from 20–23 April to clear land for a neighboring agriculture plot while simultaneously poaching. Wildlife seized included 5 pangolin parts, 8 pieces of cobra carcass, 1 civet cat head, 2 bags of fresh civet cat meat, 1 flying squirrel, and 1 jungle fowl used as a hunting decoy. All three suspects were handed to Mae Chaem Police Station.

Location: House No. 6, Moo 6, Dun Sad Subdistrict, Kranuan District, Khon Kaen
Description: Police seized 3 firearms, 1 hand grenade, and approximately 80 rounds of ammunition from a Toyota Vios vehicle when the wanted occupant, Phongsak Sai-saen (“Phong”, 37), fled on foot into the forest upon their arrival at a house in Moo 6, Dun Sad Subdistrict, Kranuan District, Khon Kaen on 23 April 2026. Phongsak was a named member of the “Pao Kranuan” drug trafficking network — whose leader had been killed in a shooting in Khao Suan Kwang District — and was subject to an outstanding arrest warrant. When officers surrounded the house, Phongsak jumped out through the rear and disappeared into the forest, leaving his girlfriend and the vehicle. Searching the Toyota Vios (which bore fake plates) officers found: 1 shotgun, 1 .22 AR-pattern assault rifle, 1 craft rifle, approximately 80 rounds of ammunition, 1 M-type fragmentation grenade (in ready-to-use condition), and 8,076 methamphetamine tablets. Investigators assessed that the network had been stockpiling weapons in anticipation of confrontations with police following the death of its leader. A pursuit operation was mounted to apprehend Phongsak.

Title: Police Seize Pistol from Drug User Who Shot Teacher in Ayutthaya

Title: Police Seize Pen Gun Used in Altercation in Sisaket

Description:

Officers then seized the pen gun from Mr. A, the 17-year-old owner, who admitted buying it via Facebook. The pistol was not recovered — suspects stated they had discarded it during flight; two rings were also unaccounted for. The suspects admitted entering Thailand as tourists, spending all their money, and planning the robbery out of desperation.

Title: Retired Police Officer Surrenders Former Service Weapon in Nakhon Sawan
Location: Moo 7, Ban Takrudphiban, Wang Nam Lat Subdistrict, Phaisali District, Nakhon Sawan
Description: Police received a 9mm pistol used by Pol Sub Lt Boonpeng, 64, a retired police officer, to shoot and kill his drug-addicted son Nattapon, 38, inside their home in Moo 7, Ban Takrudphiban, Phaisali District, Nakhon Sawan on 2 May 2026. The weapon was Boonpeng's former service firearm and was registered. Nattapon had arrived at the residence demanding money from his mother, and when refused, brandished a knife and threatened her and other household members, also attempting to seize his father's firearm. Boonpeng intervened, retrieved the pistol, and opened fire, killing Nattapon at the scene. Eight spent 9mm casings were recovered. Boonpeng called police himself and surrendered with the weapon. Boonpeng was charged with intentional homicide; however, the firearm was registered and discharged inside a private residence, meaning no separate firearms licensing offence was identified. The incident was widely reported as a case of a father acting in defense of his family.
Weapons: 1 registered 9mm pistol (former service weapon of Pol Sub Lt Boonpeng).
Actors: Former police/soldier (Pol Sub Lt Boonpeng, 64, retired); Criminal Individual (Nattapon, 38, drug user — victim, deceased); Police (Phaisali Police Station, Nakhon Sawan).

Title: Police Seize Craft Pistols from Youth Gang After Attempted Murder in Phuket
Location: Laem Dinso, Wichit Subdistrict, Mueang Phuket District, Phuket
Description: Police seized two converted pistols and ammunition when they arrested members of a youth gang known as the 'Waiyarai Gang' following a shooting at the Laem Dinso viewpoint, Wichit Subdistrict, Phuket on 5 May 2026. The gang carried out at least two separate shooting incidents that day: an attempted murder at Laem Dinso and a vehicle shooting in Kathu District. Watchari, 18, was arrested with two craft pistols, one magazine, six rounds, and a box of 24 spent .38 casings — the firearms confirmed as those used in the Laem Dinso attack. The perpetrators of the Laem Dinso shooting, Yosakorn, 17, and Naphat, 15, were subsequently arrested and remanded to the Phuket Juvenile Court on charges of joint attempted murder and carrying firearms in public. Two further juveniles, Piphat, 15, and Anuthat, 16, were arrested for the Kathu vehicle shooting using weapons from the same network. Eleven additional minors present at the scene were identified for neglect proceedings against their guardians.
Actors: Youth Gang ('Waiyarai Gang' — Watchari, 18; Yosakorn, 17; Naphat, 15; Piphat, 15; Anuthat, 16; and 11 others); Police (Wichit Police Station, Phuket Provincial Police, led by Pol Maj Gen Sinlert Sukhum; Kathu Police Station).

Title: Police Seize Craft Pistol Among 30 Weapons in Krabi Anti-Drug Sweep
Location: Moo 7, Khlong Thom Tai Subdistrict, Khlong Thom District, Krabi
Description:
Police seized one craft pistol with ammunition along with approximately 20,000 yaba tablets when they searched a residence and palm oil plantation in Moo 7, Khlong Thom Tai Subdistrict, Khlong Thom, Krabi on 5 May 2026, acting on informant intelligence. The drugs were concealed inside fuel containers buried under palm waste in the plantation. Two suspects were arrested and an Isuzu D-Max pickup was seized.

This operation was among the most significant outcomes of a multi-agency law enforcement sweep conducted between 1 April and 7 May 2026 across Krabi Province. The broader operation resulted in 224 drug vendors, 4 users, and 7 wanted persons arrested; 85,710 yaba tablets, 24.6 grams of ice, and 30 firearms (in total) seized; and 5 conspiracy cases charged. Provincial authorities noted an increase in debt-collection violence linked to drug trafficking networks in Taichelek, Myanmar.
Weapons: 5 rifles; 1 shotgun; 10 craft handguns; 14 unspecified firearms.
Actors: Criminal Organization (2 unnamed suspects — drug trafficking network linked to Taichelek, Myanmar); Police (Krabi Provincial CID, Khlong Thom Police Station, led by Pol Maj Gen Sukhatsem Nakharawilai; Krabi Provincial Administration).

Title: Police Seize Pistol From Murder Suspect After Deadly Land Dispute in Kanchanaburi
Location: Moo 7, Lat Ya Subdistrict, Mueang Kanchanaburi District, Kanchanaburi
Description:
Police seized an unlicensed 9mm Glock 17 pistol and seven rounds of ammunition after Chayant Ketkhong, 47, shot and killed two members of his ex-wife's family and wounded another in Moo 7, Lat Ya Subdistrict, Mueang Kanchanaburi on 6 May 2026. Chayant killed his former father- and brother-in-law and injured his mother-in-law. Chayant fled on foot, hid in a hillside forest approximately 3 kilometers away, and surrendered after police negotiated his standdown.

The incident was caused by a protracted dispute over sugar cane cultivation rights and property division on approximately 700 rai of farmland following a court-ordered divorce in late 2025. The ex-wife's family had allegedly failed to comply with a court order to transfer a tractor to Chayant and had left sugar cane on his portion of the land, preventing him from replanting. The suspect's daughter stated that financial pressure — specifically his inability to pledge the tractor to the sugar factory for a farming loan — also contributed to the outburst.
Actors: Criminal Individual (Chayant Ketkhong, 47 — murder suspect); Police (Lat Ya Police Station, Kanchanaburi Provincial Police, led by Pol Maj Gen Phatsawi Rueangphu).

Title: Police Seize Revolver from Drug Dealer in Ratchaburi
Location: Village 10, Nong Pho Subdistrict, Photharam District, Ratchaburi Province
Description: Police seized one revolver and ammunition when they arrested Wirapat, 29, at his home in Village 10, Nong Pho Subdistrict, Photharam District, Ratchaburi on 6 May 2026, following complaints from residents about drug activity. A search of the bedroom uncovered 5,800 yaba tablets and a revolver with ammunition on and beside the bed. Wirapat confessed to distributing yaba to Burmese migrant workers and local youth to raise money for home repairs and family expenses. He was charged with drug possession for supply, illegal firearm possession, and drug use.
Actors: Criminal Individual (Wirapat, 29 — drug dealer); Police (Photharam Police Station, Ratchaburi; Nong Pho subdistrict headman).

Title: Police Seize Craft Pistol and Improvised Grenade from Two Men in Nakhon Phanom
Location: Highway 212, Ban Na Hua Bo, Tambon At Samat, Mueang Nakhon Phanom District, Nakhon Phanom
Description: Police seized one craft handgun and one improvised hand grenade when they intercepted two men riding a motorcycle the wrong way on Highway 212 near the approach to the Third Thai-Lao Friendship Bridge on 6 May 2026. When signaled to stop, the suspects fled; the motorcycle crashed shortly thereafter. The pillion passenger, Mahaarannop, 24, fell and suffered minor head injuries. The rider, Jirakorn ('Wim'), 19, was shot three times with rubber bullets after attempting to reach into a bag — suspected by officers to be drawing a firearm. A search of the bag yielded the firearm. Mahaarannop was separately found to be carrying an improvised hand grenade in a waist bag. Both tested positive for methamphetamine. Jirakorn confessed to intending to assault people near the bridge. Jirakorn was locally known for posting videos displaying firearms on social media.
Actors: Criminal Individual (Jirakorn/Wim, 19 — perpetrator, arrested; Mahaarannop, 24 — accomplice, arrested); Police (Mueang Nakhon Phanom Police Station, patrol unit).

Title: Police Raid Illegal Gambling Den and Seize Three Firearms and Ammunition in Loei
Location: Moo 1, Ban Plaba, Tambon Plaba, Phu Ruea District, Loei
Description: Police seized three firearms and 124 rounds of ammunition when they raided an illegal gambling session at a house in Moo 1, Ban Plaba, Phu Ruea District, Loei on 7 May 2026. Five gamblers were arrested — all elderly women who were unable to flee in time. A search of the house where one gambler (identified as Nueng) had taken refuge revealed one cap rifle, one shotgun, and one .22 air rifle, along with 118 rounds of .22 ammunition and 6 shotgun shells. Nueng claimed the weapons belonged to the homeowner (her nephew) and that she was merely house-sitting. The homeowner was summoned separately to face charges of illegal firearm possession.
Actors: Criminal Individual (unnamed homeowner — summoned for illegal firearm possession); Criminal Organization (5 unnamed elderly women — illegal gambling, arrested); Police (Phu Ruea Police Station, Loei; Phu Ruea District office, led by Sit Khunsi).

Title: Wildlife Officers Seize Homemade Rifle from Poacher in Phrae National Forest
Location: Ban Tho Saman, Moo 11, Tao Poon Subdistrict, Song District, Phrae
Description: Police seized a homemade .22-calibre rifle and one round of ammunition when they arrested Atthani Saensong in Mae Song National Forest Reserve, Moo 11, Tao Poon Subdistrict, Song District, Phrae on 7 May 2026. Officers also confiscated two Bengal monitor lizards (one dead, one alive) and the suspect's unlicensed motorcycle. Atthani was charged with unlicensed hunting of a protected species, illegal possession of a protected animal carcass, illegal collection of forest products in a national reserve, unlicensed firearm possession, and carrying a firearm in a public area.
Actors: Criminal Individual (Atthani Saensong — poacher, arrested); Other State Agency (Doi Luang Wildlife Sanctuary; Conservation Area Administration Office 13, Phrae; Border Patrol Police Company 323; forestry patrol unit, National Forest Management Office 3, Phrae).

Title: Police Seize Two Firearms from Illegal Loan Shark Gang in Chonburi
Location: Huai Kaphi Subdistrict, Mueang Chonburi District, Chonburi Province
Description: Police seized two pistols and ammunition when they arrested three suspects — Kittisak, 35, Kritsada, 43, and God, 37 — in connection with an illegal high-interest loan and vehicle mortgage scheme operating through Facebook and LINE under the alias 'KT Money Car.' Acting on informant intelligence, officers conducted a buy-bust operation; the three were arrested and Kritsada led police to his home in Huai Kaphi Subdistrict, Chonburi, where two firearms and ammunition were concealed in a bedroom wardrobe. Six vehicles, 52,000 baht in cash, mobile phones, and disguised loan contracts were also seized. Suspects were charged with illegal money lending and illegal firearm possession.
Actors: Criminal Organization (Kittisak, 35; Kritsada, 43; God, 37 — loan shark gang, arrested); Police (Saen Suk Police Station, Chonburi, led by Pol Col Naratcha Udomsi).

Title: Police Seize Three Firearms and Drugs from Couple in Sakon Nakhon Farm Hut
Location: Ban Khok Kong, Moo 5, Nong Waeng Tai Subdistrict, Wanariniwat District, Sakon Nakhon
Description: Police seized three unspecified firearms and 14 rounds of ammunition when they raided a farm hut and arrested two suspects in Moo 5, Nong Waeng Tai Subdistrict, Wanariniwat District, Sakon Nakhon on 7 May 2026. A search of the hut and surrounding area yielded 724 yaba tablets, three unlicensed firearms, and 14 rounds of ammunition. Both suspects were charged with joint drug supply (yaba), drug use, and joint unlicensed firearm possession. Investigators intended to trace the weapons' origins.
Actors: Criminal Individual (Wirat/Lek, 34; Suwippha/Rot, 31 — drug trafficking couple, arrested); Police (Wanariniwat Police Station, led by Pol Col Wichan Suthammaphaeng; Sakon Nakhon).

Title: Police Seize Craft Shotgun and Drugs from Residence in Phayao
Location: Ban Pang Kha Nuea, Village No. 6, Pha Chang Noi Subdistrict, Pong District, Phayao
Description: Border Patrol personnel seized one craft shotgun and ammunition when they raided a residence in Ban Pang Kha Nuea, Moo 6, Pha Chang Noi Subdistrict, Pong District, Phayao on 7 May 2026. The male occupant fled into the surrounding forest when officers arrived; his wife was arrested. A search of the home yielded 4,334 yaba tablets — packaged in two large bundles (approximately 4,040 tablets) and a smaller pouch (294 tablets) — one craft shotgun, and additional ammunition. The wife was charged with joint drug supply and joint illegal firearm possession on the basis of shared occupancy; a warrant was sought for the male suspect.
Actors: Criminal Individual (unnamed female — drug network member, arrested; unnamed male — drug dealer, at large); Police (Border Patrol Police Company 326, led by Pol Lt Col Ukkharaphon Ritluen; Pong Police Station, Phayao).

Title: Police Seize Craft Handgun After Love Conflict in Sisaket
Location: Ban Krachaeng, Kantaralak District, Sisaket
Description: Police seized one craft handgun when they arrested a shooting suspect in Nong Ya Lat Subdistrict, Kantaralak District, Sisaket on 7 May 2026. The suspect Nathaphong, 25, had tracked the victim Somboon to a resort after discovering that he had sent Facebook messages to Nathaphong's girlfriend with romantic intent. After an argument, he drew the firearm and fired. The victim was transferred to Sisaket Hospital. Nathaphong was arrested at a residence in Ban Krachaeng, Kantaralak. He confessed, stating he had purchased the craft pistol privately from a man named 'Kook' in Prachinburi Province for THB 5,000. He was charged with attempted murder, illegal firearm possession, and carrying a firearm in public.
Actors: Criminal Individual (Nathaphong, 25 — shooting suspect); Police (Kantaralak Police Station, Sisaket).

Title: Pistol and Shotgun Seized Over Feud Linked to Criminal Activities in Nakhon Si Thammarat
Location: Taling Chan Subdistrict, Tha Sala District, Nakhon Si Thammarat
Description: Police seized a registered shotgun and pistol during investigations into a deadly dispute between Atthaphong Srisuwon and Phuriwat, 40, a locally influential figure, in Taling Chan Subdistrict, Tha Sala District, Nakhon Si Thammarat on 7 May 2026. Atthaphong shot and killed Phuriwat with a shotgun as he rode a motorcycle on a road. The victim — who reportedly had a prior criminal record, including drug cases — had previously posted online challenges to rivals and had allegedly attacked Atthaphong at least twice before. A registered 11mm pistol was found holstered at the victim's waist at the scene, indicating he had been armed. Atthaphong surrendered to Tha Sala Police Station on 8 May 2026; a search of his premises found no additional weapons. A registered shotgun was discovered at the victim's home and seized for forensic testing. Police noted a possible connection to local criminal business activities.
Actors: Criminal Individual (Atthaphong Srisuwon/At Taling Chan — perpetrator, surrendered; Phuriwat/Nam Plakpla, 40 — victim, deceased, prior drug record); Police (Tha Sala Police Station, Nakhon Si Thammarat).

Title: Park Rangers Seize Firearms from Poachers in Chiang Mai
Location: Huai Pha Cho Forest, Srilanna National Park, Phrao District, Chiang Mai Province
Description: Park rangers seized two firearms and ammunition when they intercepted three poachers in a longtail boat on the Mae Ngat Reservoir in the park's Huai Pha Cho forest zone of Srilanna National Park on 8 May 2026. Rangers spotted a suspicious longtail boat moored on the bank, set up a surveillance position, and intercepted Wattana, Jan, and Boonrit — all residents of Phrao District — as they headed toward Phrao in the vessel. A search yielded one shotgun, one air rifle, ammunition, and a walkie-talkie radio. All three confessed they had entered the park to hunt wildlife. The boat was also confiscated. Suspects were transferred to Mae Taeng Police Station for prosecution.
Actors: Criminal Individual (Wattana, Jan, Boonrit — poachers, arrested); Other State Agency (Srilanna National Park SMART Patrol, Conservation Area Administration Office 16 (Chiang Mai)).

Title: Police Seize Shotgun from Man Who Shot Ex-Wife in Suphanburi
Location: Moo 6, Bang Len Subdistrict, Song Phi Nong District, Suphanburi
Description: Police seized a shotgun when they arrested Suchat, 54, who shot his recently divorced wife Pornthip, 37, three times in the neck inside their bedroom in Moo 6, Bang Len Subdistrict, Song Phi Nong, Suphanburi on 9 May 2026. The incident occurred one day after the couple formalized their divorce. The suspect had a history of jealousy and alcohol-related quarrels. He was charged with intentional homicide.
Actors: Criminal Individual (Suchat, 54 — murder suspect); Police (Song Phi Nong Police Station, Suphanburi).

Title: Police Seize .38 Colt Revolver from Drug Dealer in Bangkok
Location: Wat Phraya Krai Area, Bang Kho Laem District, Bangkok
Description: Police seized one .38-calibre revolver with 58 rounds of ammunition when they arrested Boonming, 45, alias 'Ming Phra Pradaeng,' a suspected drug dealer, following a buy-bust operation in Bang Kho Laem District, Bangkok on 9 May 2026. After an undercover purchase confirmed Boonming was selling narcotics in Phra Pradaeng, Samut Prakan, officers traced him to a meeting point in Bang Kho Laem where the purchase was completed. A consented search of his room yielded 1,846 yaba tablets, approximately 98.93 grams of ice.
Actors: Criminal Individual (Boonming, 45, alias 'Ming Phra Pradaeng' — drug dealer, arrested); Police (Metropolitan Investigation Bureau, Investigation Sub-Division 4, Bangkok).

Title: Government Forces Seize Craft Pistol from Fleeing Drug Smugglers in Chiang Mai
Location: Tambon Mae Ai, Mae Ai District, Chiang Mai
Description: Government forces seized one craft pistol along with 191 yaba tablets and approximately 57.6 kilograms of raw opium when they intercepted a group of 3-5 drug smugglers crossing the Myanmar border at a natural passage in Mae Ai District, Chiang Mai on 9 May 2026 following an intelligence tip. When officers signaled a halt, the group opened fire with unidentified weapons, triggering a five-minute firefight. The group then fled exploiting terrain familiarity, abandoning four backpacks containing the opium (36 blocks averaging 1.6 kg each), one craft pistol, spent shotgun casings, and a radio transceiver. No personnel casualties were reported on the security forces' side.
Actors: Criminal Organization (3-5 unidentified drug smuggling group, at large — suspected transnational network); Military (Royal Thai Army Ranger Company 3207; Pha Mueang Long-Range Patrol Unit; Pha Mueang Task Force, Army Area 3 Command, led by Maj Gen Sathit Waiyonon).

Title: Authorities Seize Craft Pistol and Drugs from Four Suspects at Checkpoint in Loei
Location: Ban Huai Thap Chang, Moo 7, Pak Chom Subdistrict, Pak Chom District, Loei
Description: Government forces seized one craft pistol and 176 yaba tablets when they stopped a white sedan at a checkpoint in Moo 7, Pak Chom Subdistrict, Pak Chom District, Loei on 10 May 2026. All four male occupants displayed suspicious behavior; a search of the vehicle yielded the craft pistol and ammunition concealed in a waist bag on the passenger seat alongside the yaba. All four were arrested and transferred to Pak Chom Police Station, charged with joint drug possession and joint illegal firearm possession.
Actors: Criminal Organization (4 unnamed male suspects — drug distribution, arrested); Military (Ranger Company 2109, Surasak Montri Task Force); Police (Pak Chom Police Station, Loei; local volunteer defence forces).

Title: Police Seize Pistol from Love Conflict Suspect in Chonburi
Location: Soi Nongyai 8, Na Kluea Subdistrict, Bang Lamung District, Chonburi
Description: Police seized a .380-calibre Colt pistol and four rounds of ammunition when they arrested Kitti ('Peem'), 32, for killing Apichai ('Nui') Maenmueang, 34, a restaurant owner, in Soi Sukhumvit Pattaya 15/12, Bang Lamung District, Chonburi on 10 May 2026. The suspect coerced his girlfriend, a former close acquaintance of the victim, to send chat messages luring the victim into an ambush. Kitti's accomplices Panapphon ('Nik'), 34, and Phongsat ('Niew'), 33, were also in the vehicle. All four suspects surrendered after sustained investigative pressure the same day, and Kitti led police to the firearm, which was concealed at a residence in Soi Nongyai 8, Na Kluea Subdistrict.
Actors: Criminal Individual (Kitti/Peem, 32 — perpetrator, surrendered; Panapphon/Nik, 34; Phongsat/Niew, 33 — accomplices, surrendered; Siriwimol/An-An, 24 — accomplice, surrendered; Apichai/Nui Maenmueang, 34 — victim, deceased); Police (Bang Lamung Police Station, Chonburi, led by Pol Col Sarawut Nuchnart).

Title: Armed Group Intimidates Officials at Protected Beach in Phuket
Location: Khao Nak Koet National Reserve Forest, Karon Subdistrict, Mueang Phuket / Pa Tong Subdistrict, Kathu District, Phuket
Description:
An armed group used firearms to intimidate locals near Freedom Beach, Phuket over a period spanning January 2025 to May 2026. The intimidation campaign prompted more than 100 forestry and administrative officials led by Phuket Deputy Governor Rungrueng Thimabut to inspect the area following community complaints of illegal forest clearing, resort construction, road building, and tourist infrastructure on 24 March 2026.

Agents arrested five suspects for encroaching on protected forest; one additional suspect faced an illegal firearm possession charge. Sections of the protected forest had been cleared, electrified, and developed into accommodation, shops, and a staircase of approximately 300 steps — reportedly sold in parcels to foreign nationals for upwards of 18 million baht. Four elephants were found chained and removed. By 13 May, 23 cases covering over 58 rai had been filed with 17 persons arrested.

A central figure, Parinthorn ('Sia Lek'), 51, surrendered to police on 13 May 2026 to acknowledge four cases of reserve forest encroachment covering approximately 10 rai. He denied being 'influential' but confirmed owning four registered firearms and claimed prior assassination attempts had led him to arm himself for self-defense. An online threatening post, attributed to a suspect identified by the initial 'L,' stated that shooting an MP would incur only THB 200,000 bail — interpreted as a threat to Phuket constituency 2 People's Party MP Chalermpong Saengdee.

Images of the armed group showed one AR-pattern assault rifle and three shotguns.
Actor Type:
Criminal Organization
Civilian Official
Other State Agency
Police
Actors: Criminal Organization (Freedom Beach encroachment network, including Parinthorn/Sia Lek and unidentified associates — arrested and summoned for prosecution); Civilian Official (MP Chalermpong Saengdee — target of threats); Other State Agency (Royal Forest Department; Phuket Forestry Centre; Karon and Patong municipalities); Police (Karon Police Station; Phuket Provincial Police, led by Pol Maj Gen Nuntachat Supamongkhol); Civilian Official (Phuket Deputy Governor Rungrueng Thimabut; PM Anutin Charnvirakul).

Title: Police Seize Shotgun from Agitated Drug User in Phetchabun
Location: Sadapong Subdistrict, Khao Kho District, Phetchabun
Description: Police seized one shotgun and four rounds of ammunition when they arrested Wasan, 37, on a public road in Sadapong Subdistrict, Khao Kho District, Phetchabun on 11 May 2026. Wasan had been discharging the shotgun inside the village in an agitated state — reportedly after consuming yaba — and created panic among residents. Post-arrest testing confirmed methamphetamine in his system.
Actors: Criminal Individual (Wasan, 37 — perpetrator, arrested); Police (Khao Kho Police Station, Phetchabun Provincial SWAT, Provincial CID, led by Pol Col Boonthueng Mueangsaeng).

Title: M16 Assault Rifle Found on Former Police Officer Killed in Nakhon Si Thammarat
Location: Moo 12, Kamlon Subdistrict, Lanska District, Nakhon Si Thammarat Province
Description: Police found an M16 assault rifle on the body of Surachet ('Nueng') Bunyued, 38, a former police sub-lieutenant dismissed from service five years prior, after he was killed at his remote mangosteen orchard in Moo 12, Kamlon Subdistrict, Lan Saka District, Nakhon Si Thammarat on 12 May 2026. The victim was found by relatives the morning after his death. The rifle was believed to be his own firearm, which he routinely carried while tending the orchard. The location required a 6-7 km motorbike ascent on steep terrain and was without mobile coverage, complicating the investigation. Police identified the perpetrator and initiated a warrant process within hours of discovery; the killing is believed to stem from a chronic boundary dispute with a close relative over farmland.
Actors: Criminal Individual (unidentified relative — perpetrator, at large; arrest warrant sought); Former police/soldier (Surachet Bunyued, 38, former police sub-lieutenant — victim, deceased); Police (Lanska Police Station, Nakhon Si Thammarat Provincial CID).

Title: Troops Seize Craft Pistol from Another Fleeing Drug Caravan in Chiang Mai
Location: Ban Pang Nai, Moo 1, Mali Ka Subdistrict, Mae Ai District, Chiang Mai
Description: Troops seized one craft pistol along with approximately 1,080,000 yaba tablets and 10.8-12.8 kilograms of raw opium when they intercepted a drug smuggling caravan of 5-10 persons crossing the Myanmar border on natural trails in Mae Ai District, Chiang Mai during the night of 10-11 May 2026. The group opened fire on soldiers; a five-minute exchange ensued before they fled, abandoning seven backpacks loaded with yaba and opium. Four spent casings were recovered with the craft pistol. No security forces casualties were reported. The incident followed a similar encounter the day before.
Actors: Criminal Organization (5-10 unidentified drug smuggling caravan, at large); Military (Ranger Company 3206; Pha Mueang Long-Range Patrol Unit; Pha Mueang Task Force, led by Maj Gen Sathit Waiyonon).

Title: Highway Police Seize Revolver from Speeding Motorist in Nakhon Ratchasima
Location: Mittraphap Road, Klang Dong Subdistrict, Pak Chong District, Nakhon Ratchasima
Description: Police seized one .32-calibre revolver and two rounds of ammunition when they stopped a speeding sedan driving on Mittraphap Road (outbound Bangkok direction), Klang Dong Subdistrict, Pak Chong District, Nakhon Ratchasima on 12 May 2026. The driver, a 48-year-old man, consented to a search of the vehicle; officers found the revolver in a black bag along with two rounds and a holster. The suspect confessed the firearm was his and was charged with carrying a firearm in public without authorization.
Actors: Police (Nakhon Ratchasima Highway Police).

Title: Police Seize Two Pistols from Suspected Drug Dealers at Hotel in Phetchaburi
Location: Mueang District, Phetchaburi Province
Description: Police seized two pistols along with over 10,300 yaba tablets and 23.59 grams of crystal meth when they arrested a male suspect, 35, and a female suspect, 39, in a buy-bust operation in front of a hotel in Mueang District, Phetchaburi Province on 12 May 2026. A search of the suspects and their vehicle yielded a .45mm STI Spartan III pistol (worth approximately 70,000 baht), a 9mm Norinco pistol (worth approximately 40,000 baht), 10,000 yaba tablets (from the controlled purchase), 300 additional yaba tablets, 23.59 grams of ice, cash, jewellery, and a black Nissan Almera sedan. Both suspects tested positive for methamphetamine. The male suspect additionally faced charges of driving while under the influence of narcotics and carrying a firearm in public.
Weapons: 1 .45-calibre STI Spartan III pistol; 1 9mm Norinco pistol.
Actors: Criminal Individual (drug dealers); Police (Mueang Phetchaburi Police Station CID, led by Pol Maj Gen Kitti Suksomlak, Phetchaburi PPC; Pol Col Chokchai Niaelssen, station commander).

Title: Border Security Personnel Seize Craft Rifle from Drug Caravan in Chiang Mai
Location: Natural border crossing, Ban Rom Thai Area, Moo 14, Tha Ton Subdistrict, Mae Ai District, Chiang Mai
Description: Border security personnel seized one craft rifle and killed a suspected smuggler when they intercepted a drug smuggling group crossing the Myanmar border near Ban Rom Thai, Mae Ai District, Chiang Mai on 12 May 2026. When signaled to halt, the group opened fire; a firefight ensued. At dawn on 13 May, security forces cleared the contact area and found one body carrying a backpack and clutching a homemade rifle. Two additional backpacks were found nearby, containing approximately 250,000 yaba tablets and 11.2 kilograms of raw opium. The deceased was believed to be a foreign national who had illegally crossed into Thailand. Evidence suggested the group had been awaiting vehicle pickup. The incident followed two similar encounters between 9-11 May.
Actors: Criminal Organization (drug smuggling group — 1 killed, others at large; suspected foreign nationals); Military (Ranger Company 3209; Pha Mueang Long-Range Patrol Unit; Pha Mueang Task Force, led by Maj Gen Sathit Waiyonon).

Title: Police Seize Craft Handgun from Drug Dealer After Standoff in Chiang Rai
Location: Ban Kao Lang, Moo 10, Pa Sang Subdistrict, Mae Chan District, Chiang Rai
Description: Police seized one craft handgun and ammunition when they arrested Aja Kala, 54, a major drug dealer, at a property in Ban Kao Lang, Moo 10, Pa Sang Subdistrict, Mae Chan, Chiang Rai on 12 May 2026. Aja drew a firearm and fired at officers to escape, then fled on foot to a neighboring house occupied by elderly and a young child, where he barricaded himself in an outdoor bathroom. A three-hour standoff followed, with police surrounding the area and calling in the 191 patrol unit; local community leaders, and ultimately Aja's wife and son, were brought to negotiate. Aja expressed extreme stress and requested a final use of heroin before surrendering, laying down the firearm and emerging with hands raised. A search of the area yielded the craft pistol, 8 rounds of ammunition, one modified 9mm barrel, 201 yaba tablets, 34.5 grams of heroin, and approximately 2 kilograms of liquid heroin.
Actors: Criminal Organization (Aja Kala, 54 — major drug dealer, arrested); Police (Mae Chan Police Station, Chiang Rai; 191 patrol unit; Chiang Rai Provincial Police, led by Pol Maj Gen Manop Senakul).

Title: Police Seize Handguns After Deadly Love Conflict in Nakhon Pathom
Location: Sanam Chan Subdistrict, Mueang Nakhon Pathom District, Nakhon Pathom
Description: Police seized a pistol and a revolver after a kamnan (subdistrict headman) shot and killed a pig slaughterhouse owner following a dispute outside a nightclub in Sanam Chan Subdistrict, Mueang Nakhon Pathom on 12 May 2026. Komsak Kaewsang, kamnan of Don Yai Hom, shot Udomphong Senbua, 54, in the head after he encountered Udomphong — who was now in a relationship with Komsak's former fifth wife — at the nightclub, and that Udomphong had insulted her repeatedly. The revolver was seized from the victim's vehicle and may have belonged to him.
Actors: Civilian Official (Komsak Kaewsang/Kamnan Jiw — kamnan of Don Yai Hom, perpetrator, surrendered); Police (Mueang Nakhon Pathom Police Station, led by Pol Maj Gen Phithak Upphong).

Title: Police Seize Sig Sauer Pistol from Drug Dealers in Kanchanaburi
Location: Ban Rang Kratai, Takram-en Subdistrict, Tha Maka District, Kanchanaburi
Description: Police seized one Sig Sauer P365 9mm pistol and 11 rounds of ammunition when they arrested Thanaphat, 48, and two co-suspects at a rented room in Ban Rang Kratai, Takram-en Subdistrict, Tha Maka District, Kanchanaburi on 12 May 2026. A search of the room yielded the pistol alongside 2,447 yaba tablets distributed across all three arrestees: 2,200 on Thanaphat, 180 on Chamnan, 50, and 67 on Sompong, 41. Sompong was additionally wanted under an arrest warrant from Sakon Nakhon Provincial Court for a computer crime offence.
Weapons: 1 Sig Sauer P365 9mm pistol; 11 rounds of ammunition.
Actors: Criminal Individual (suspected drug dealers, arrested); Police (Tha Ruea Police Station, Kanchanaburi, led by Pol Col Techint Banchong; Pol Lt Col Amnat Preechawat; Pol Lt Col Krisawit Khaokhao).

Title: Police Seize Pistol from Money Mule Network Leader in Chanthaburi
Location: 176/10 Moo 10, Tha Chang Subdistrict, Mueang District, Chanthaburi
Description: Police seized one unregistered Norinco 9mm semi-automatic pistol and eight rounds of ammunition when they searched and arrested Natit ("Ball Tha Chang"), 27, at his home in Moo 10, Tha Chang Subdistrict, Mueang District, Chanthaburi on 12 May 2026. Natit was suspected of directing a transnational network that procured and sold bank accounts used in cybercrime. The search, conducted under Chanthaburi Provincial Court warrant No. 58/2569 (dated 12 May 2026), also yielded one "PotKe" brand e-cigarette pod mixed with ketamine and THB 1.5 million in cash. The case expanded from a February 2025 operation in which two associates were arrested alongside 429 bank passbooks, 397 ATM cards, 259 SIM cards, and 68 mobile phones; Natit was subsequently identified as ringleader. He was charged with receiving or possessing property connected to a customs offence, unlawful possession of a controlled psychotropic substance (Class 2 — ketamine), and illegal firearm and ammunition possession, and was transferred to Mueang Chanthaburi Police Station.
Weapons: 1 Norinco 9mm semi-automatic pistol; 8 rounds of ammunition.
Actors: Criminal Organization (Natit / "Ball Tha Chang", 27 — money mule network leader, arrested); Police (Chanthaburi Provincial CID, led by Pol Col Arkhaphong Sunthrawiphat, Pol Col Pheerapon Selarath, and Pol Lt Col Tanaded Labmala; Mueang Chanthaburi Police Station).

Title: Police Seize .380 Pistol from Online Seller in Chonburi Sting
Location: Moo 4, Ban Kao Subdistrict, Phan Thong District, Chonburi
Description: Police seized one .380-calibre pistol when they arrested Chalerm Chaemchamrat, 30, following a buy-bust operation in Wat Ban Kao, Moo 4, Ban Kao Subdistrict, Phan Thong District, Chonburi on 13 May 2026. CCIB officers had detected Chalerm advertising the pistol for sale at THB 10,000 in a Facebook group for illegal firearms trade. After posing as buyers and arranging a meeting, officers arrested Chalerm on delivery of the weapon. He confessed to having purchased the pistol online for THB 5,000 and reselling it for profit to raise money for his five children. He was charged with illegal firearm possession and carrying a firearm in a public area.
Actors: Criminal Individual (Chalerm Chaemchamrat, 30 — online gun dealer, arrested); Police (Cyber Investigation Bureau CCIB Sub-Division 1, led by Pol Maj Gen Siriwat Di-pho and Pol Lt Gen Surapon Prembutr).

Title: Police Seize Five Pistols from Storage Locker of Swedish National in Phuket
Location: Mespace Self Storage Patong, Jungceylon Shopping Centre, 181 Rat-U-Thit 200 Pi Road, Patong Subdistrict, Kathu District, Phuket
Description: Police seized four Glock 9mm pistols, one CZ 9mm pistol, and 100 rounds of ammunition from storage locker No. 2184 at Mespace Self Storage Patong inside Jungceylon shopping centre, Patong, Phuket, on 13 May 2026. The locker had been rented under the name of Elmi Ahmed Mohamed, a Swedish national, who had defaulted on payment for more than 120 days and could not be contacted. The storage company opened the locker on 12 May in accordance with company procedures upon discovering the firearms, which are prohibited items under its storage rules, and immediately notified police. Patong police inspected the locker on 13 May, confirmed all five pistols bore registration numbers, and seized all weapons and ammunition pending investigation of their provenance and lawful possession status. Mohamed had not been located as of 14 May 2026 and the investigation remained ongoing.
Weapons: 4 Glock 9mm pistols; 1 CZ 9mm pistol; 100 rounds of 9mm ammunition.
Actors: Criminal individual (Elmi Ahmed Mohamed, Swedish national — locker lessee, absconded, identity confirmed by storage company records); Police (Patong Police Station, led by Pol Col Korakrit Khanakrua; Phuket Provincial Police, led by Pol Maj Gen Sinlert Sukhum).

Title: Police Seize Revolver from Drug Dealer in Sa Kaeo
Location: Wang Sombun District, Sa Kaeo
Description: Police seized an unregistered revolver along with more than 400 yaba tablets and an unspecified quantity of crystal meth when they arrested a drug dealer known as "Jo Phornimit" in Wang Sombun District, Sa Kaeo, on 13 May 2026. The arrest was conducted by Wang Sombun Police Station's investigation unit following a planned sting operation.
Actors: Criminal Individual ("Jo Phornimit" — drug dealer, arrested); Police (Wang Sombun Police Station, Sa Kaeo).

Title: Cyber Police Seize Pistol from Man Who Advertised Gun on Facebook in Rayong
Location: Tambon Phae, Amphoe Mueang Rayong, Rayong Province
Description: Cyber police seized one unregistered .380-calibre semi-automatic pistol and five rounds of ammunition when they arrested Chaiyon, 27, outside his rented room in Tambon Phae, Mueang Rayong District, Rayong on 14 May 2026. The operation was initiated after Cyber Crime Division officers detected a Facebook Story showing the firearm alongside a threatening caption; the account was linked to Chaiyon through technical investigation. When officers arrived, Chaiyon attempted to flee but was quickly apprehended. A search of his bag yielded the pistol and ammunition alongside 116 yaba tablets and 16 grams of crystal meth. Chaiyon admitted he had obtained the firearm from a friend in Rayong and planned to sell it for THB 9,000. The drugs had been separately ordered from another Facebook contact for re-sale. Chaiyon was charged with illegal firearm possession, carrying a firearm in public, and possession of controlled substances (yaba and ice) with intent to supply.
Actors: Criminal Individual (suspected online arms and drugs seller); Police (Cyber Crime Investigation Bureau Division 1 (บก.สอท.1), led by Pol Lt Gen Surapon Prembutr and Pol Maj Gen Siriwat Di-pho; Pol Col Kritin Tapasilo).

Title: Police Seize Converted Revolver at Checkpoint in Nakhon Ratchasima
Location: Pho Klang Subdistrict, Mueang Nakhon Ratchasima District, Nakhon Ratchasima
Description: Police seized one converted revolver and 21 rounds of mixed ammunition at a checkpoint in Pho Klang Subdistrict, Mueang Nakhon Ratchasima, on 15 May 2026. The suspect was charged with unlawful possession of a craft firearm and unlicensed possession of ammunition.
Actors: Criminal Individual (Chinawat, surname withheld — arrested); Police (Pho Klang Police Station, Nakhon Ratchasima, led by Pol Col Patcharodanai Karin and Pol Lt Col Suthep Ramsonthia).

Title: Police Arrest British National as Gun Supplier in Phuket Assassination Case
Location: Nong Prue Subdistrict, Bang Lamung District, Chonburi
Description:
Police arrested Amir, 33, a British national, at a condominium in Nong Prue Subdistrict, Bang Lamung District, Chonburi on 15 May 2026, on charges of supplying firearms used in the premeditated assassination of an Iraqi tourist in Phuket on 7 February 2026. Police arrested Amir on charges of abetting premeditated murder, joint illegal possession of firearms and ammunition, carrying firearms in public without authorization, and abetting reckless discharge of a firearm in a community area. Several items believed linked to the crime were seized. Amir denied all charges and was transferred to Patong Police Station for prosecution.
The underlying incident occurred on 7 February 2026 when Colombian national Fernando Estaid Guevara Sanchez, 39, shot and killed Ameer Munder Mahmoud, 24, an Iraqi national, on Sirirat Road, Patong, Phuket. Guevara Sanchez was arrested on 8 February 2026 near Phuket International Airport while attempting to flee; police seized two firearms and ammunition. Investigators concluded that a group of foreign nationals had procured and supplied the murder weapon, that the killing was a contract assassination, and that the motive was a conflict within an international drug cartel.
Actors: Criminal Organization (Amir, 33, British national — arrested, alleged gun supplier, denied charges; Fernando Estaid Guevara Sanchez, 39, Colombian national — arrested 8 February 2026, alleged gunman; Ameer Munder Mahmoud, 24, Iraqi national — victim, deceased); Police (Crime Suppression Division (CSD/บก.ป.), led by Pol Lt Gen Nattasak Chaonasai and Pol Maj Gen Pattanasak Bupasuwan; Pol Col Thanawat Hinyokhin; Pol Lt Col Tanis Huaiwechsat; Patong Police Station, Phuket).

Title: Park Rangers Seize Firearms from Poachers in Khao Yai National Park
Location: Unit 9 (Sai Yai) Zone, Khao Yai National Park, Na Di District, Prachinburi
Description: Khao Yai National Park rangers seized one shotgun, one craft rifle, and ammunition when they arrested Thanaphon, 51, and his stepson Chumphae, 27, both of Na Di District, Prachinburi, while the pair were carrying 17 protected wildlife carcasses out of the park on 15 May 2026. The rangers had monitored the suspects for some time prior to the operation and set up an ambush following a tip-off. The seized carcasses comprised 15 greater mouse-deer, and 2 male striped civets. Thanaphon admitted it was the pair's first poaching trip and stated they had acted out of severe poverty, intending to sell the animals locally for approximately THB 100–300 per carcass. Both were charged under the National Parks Act B.E. 2562, Wildlife Conservation and Protection Act B.E. 2562 (possession of protected animal carcasses; hunting in a national park), and the Firearms Act (maximum 10 years' imprisonment), and were transferred to Na Di Police Station.
Weapons: 1 shotgun; 1 craft rifle; 5 rounds of ammunition.
Actors: Criminal Individual (poachers, arrested); Other State Agency (Khao Yai National Park Unit 9 Sai Yai rangers; Conservation Area Administration Office 1 (Prachinburi), directed by Yotsawat Thiansawat; Park Director Chaiya Huaihongthong; Zone Manager Ekawit Thoedkiatikul).

Location: Khukhan District, Sisaket
Description: Police seized one shotgun, 44 rounds of ammunition, and 6.93 grams of heroin when they arrested one suspect in Khukhan District, Sisaket on 15 May 2026. The suspect was charged with unlawful possession of a Class 1 narcotic (heroin), unlicensed possession of firearms and ammunition, unlicensed sale of pharmaceutical products, and possession of Class 2 narcotics (codeine and morphine) for supply, and was transferred to Khukhan Police Station. Images also showed an Ekol ES P95B air pistol, possibly converted to fire 9mm ammunition.
Weapons: 1 shotgun; 1 presumably converted Ekol ES P95B pistol; 44 rounds of ammunition.

Title: Police Seize Pistol after Spousal Murder-Suicide in Satun
Location: Moo 9, Thung Nui Subdistrict, Khuan Kalong District, Satun
Description: Police recovered a 9mm pistol after Jop, 60, a rubber and durian farmer, fatally shot his ex-wife Somsri, 58, at her rented home in Moo 9, Thung Nui Subdistrict, Khuan Kalong, Satun on 16 May 2026. He then returned to his own home and shot himself in the head. Somsri — a community health volunteer chairwoman and women's group chairwoman — was sitting behind her house when the suspect entered and opened fire; she died of her wounds in transit to hospital. Police and forensic teams found Jop deceased inside his home with the 9mm pistol and a spent casing nearby, alongside a suicide note stating "if I can't have her, no one can." The couple had been legally divorced and shared two adult children; the village headman stated the pair's disputes had persisted for over 18 months. Jop had a prior attempted murder conviction in 2010 and a prior necklace-snatching case involving Somsri in 2025.
Actors: Criminal Individual (Jop, 60 — perpetrator, deceased); Police

Title: Police Seize Craft Handgun in Yasothon
Location: Samran Subdistrict, Mueang Yasothon District, Yasothon
Description: Police seized one craft handgun from one suspect and 482 yaba tablets from two other suspects during separate arrests in Mueang Yasothon District on 16 May 2026. In the first arrest, officers detained "Off" (นายอ๊อฟ), 36, of Tambon Samran, and seized one firearm on charges of unlicensed possession.
Actors: Criminal Individual (illegal firearm owner); Police (Mueang Yasothon Police Station, led by Pol Col Prasit Phetrat; arresting team led by Pol 2nd Lt Sawat Sithiphai, Pol Staff Sgt Pongsak Sudaeng, Pol Tech Sgt Nipol Maisee, and Pol Sub Lt Phichwat Saensuk).

Title: Pistols Seized from Police Officers Arrested for Extorting Chinese Nationals in Sa Kaeo
Location: Moo 1, Wang Mai Subdistrict, Wang Somboon District, Sa Kaeo
Description:
Four semi-automatic pistols and 24 rounds of ammunition were seized when multi-agency officers rescued five Chinese nationals being held captive and extorted by four police officers and one civilian accomplice in Moo 1, Wang Mai Subdistrict, Wang Somboon District, Sa Kaeo on 16 May 2026. The four officers — Pol Sgt Phawin Prayat-ngern, 43 (Highway Police Division); Pol Sgt Wutthikorn Wongthom, 39 (Khlong Hat Police Station, Sa Kaeo); Pol Sgt Somchai Yukhong, 45 (Khlong Hat Police Station); and Pol Sgt Phichet Sengsun, 41 (Ban Plaeng Police Station, Chanthaburi) — along with civilian Natthanant, 43, arrested the five Chinese nationals for immigration violations, then transported them to the private address rather than processing them legally.

Police handcuffed, threatened, and demanded THB 300,000 – 2.5 million (approx. USD 10,000 – 50,000; sources disagree) each from the victims for release. Two victims transferred THB 120,000 (USD 4,000) in cryptocurrency before an immigration officer relayed a LINE distress signal to rescue teams. A fifth officer, Pol Lt Prayoon (Khlong Hat Police Station), was identified as a participant but had left to buy food at the time of the raid and subsequently surrendered voluntarily; he was charged with the same offences. All four officers were suspended from service pending disciplinary proceedings.

Police were also investigating the victims due to their frequent crossings between Thailand and Cambodia for links to scam groups and Chinese criminal organizations.
Actor Type:
Criminal Organization
Police
Other State Agency
Military
Actors: Criminal organization (rogue police officers extorting Chinese nationals; possible Chinese criminal organization involved in Cambodian scam centers); Police (Wang Somboon Police Station); Other State Agency (Sa Kaeo Immigration; Provincial Administration Special Forces); Military (Eastern Territorial Army Command)

Title: Police Seize Firearm from Robbery Gang with Local Officials in Chumphon
Location: 221 Moo 17, Tambon Rab Ro, Tha Sae District, Chumphon
Description:
Police seized one .45-caliber pistol and 19 rounds of ammunition when they arrested two members of a five-person gang that had impersonated police officers during an armed robbery of Myanmar migrant workers at 221 Moo 17, Tambon Rab Ro, Tha Sae District, Chumphon, on 17 May 2026. Five men arrived in a black four-door Toyota pickup without license plates, dressed in police-style uniforms and armed, claiming to conduct a drug inspection; they then detained five workers and stole a safe containing over THB 100,000 in cash, jewelry, and documents.

Anuchit (Anucha Chachai / "Not"), 32, a reserve Or Sor (auxiliary police) member, was arrested on 18 May and admitted driving the vehicle; he named Santhipol ("Kla"), 34, Chaloempol Saewanna ("May"), 35, and Ram Muad Phakdi, 41, assistant village headman of Moo 1, Tambon Song Phi Nong, as co-perpetrators. Santhipol was arrested in Lamae District and found in possession of the .45 pistol and ammunition. Chaloempol surrendered voluntarily on 19 May, claiming he was unaware of the robbery plan. Ram surrendered on 20 May; he denied prior knowledge, but police stated his account contradicted physical evidence. A fifth suspect remained at large. Recovered goods included 41 rings, 9 necklaces, 24 pairs of earrings, and THB 25,400 cash per arrestee's share.

Description: Border Patrol Police seized one unregistered shotgun and seven rounds of ammunition when they arrested Chaiya ("Prem," surname withheld), 47, of Moo 12, Tambon Tha Wang Thong, Mueang Phayao, in Nam Bo Sai Forest, Moo 5, Ban Tham Subdistrict, Dok Kham Tai, Phayao on 19 May 2026. Description: Immigration and border patrol police seized one handgun, five rounds of ammunition, and approximately 100,000 yaba tablets when they arrested Watcharachai, 51, near the Phu Ang Monastery, Khemarat Subdistrict, Khemarat District, Ubon Ratchathani on 19 May 2026. Title: Police Seize Grenade from Golden Triangle Drug Trafficking Network in Pathum Thani
Location: Prachatipat Subdistrict, Thanyaburi District, Pathum Thani (and Khu Khot Subdistrict, Lam Luk Ka District, Pathum Thani)
Description:
Police seized one .38-calibre revolver, six rounds of ammunition, and one RGD-5 grenade alongside approximately 8.9 million yaba tablets (estimated street value THB 300 million) when they arrested four members of the "Pao KB" (Khlong Song, Lam Luk Ka) drug network at two safe houses in Pathum Thani on 20 May 2026.

Acting on informant intelligence about a drug shipment from Saraburi Province, officers from Region 1 Narcotics Suppression Unit, Saraburi Provincial Police CID, and military intelligence from the Special Warfare Command first located approximately 8,000,000 tablets in 40 sacks at an unnumbered address in Pan Thong Niwet 3 Village, Khu Khot, Lam Luk Ka. Follow-on searches led to a further 900,000 tablets in 5 sacks at 20 Moo 3, Tambon Prachatipat, Thanyaburi, along with the revolver, grenade, and ammunition.

The four suspects — Wiroj Phattikul ("Pao"), 42; Watcharaphong Sukhong Ngwa ("Tong"), 31; Prichit Siriketu ("Bee"), 21; and Panatda Onsouna ("Pai"), a Lao national — admitted storing the drugs as a staging depot, awaiting distribution instructions, at THB 100,000 per delivery. Suspects stated the drugs originated from northern Thailand. The revolver, grenade, and associated ammunition were seized from Wiroj.
Weapons: 1 .38-calibre revolver; 6 rounds of ammunition; 1 RGD-5 hand grenade.

Title: Police Seize Two Pistols from Illegal Pawnbroker in Chanthaburi
Location: 1/3 Moo 1, Song Phi Nong Subdistrict, Tha Mai District, Chanthaburi
Description: Police seized one CZ 9mm pistol, one Glock 9mm pistol, and 16 rounds of ammunition from Prasitchai Satphanit ("Mud"), 65, at his home in Song Phi Nong Subdistrict, Tha Mai District, Chanthaburi on 20 May 2026. Prasitchai admitted to operating an unlicensed pawnbroking and informal moneylending business for seven to eight years, charging monthly interest of 5–10 per cent. Officers executing Chanthaburi Provincial Court search warrant No. 70/2569 also recovered eleven vehicles (six cars and five motorcycles) accepted as collateral, and a client ledger with registration details and debt records. Police were additionally examining possible links between Prasitchai's clientele and a local drug trafficking network. All seized items and the suspect were transferred to Thung Benchha Police Station.
Weapons: 1 CZ 9mm pistol; 1 Glock 9mm pistol; 16 rounds ammunition.
Actors: Criminal Organization (Prasitchai Satphanit/"Mud", 65 — illicit loan business); Police (Chanthaburi Provincial Police CID (กก.สส.ภ.จว.จันทบุรี), led by Pol Col Pheeraphon Selarath, Pol Lt Col Thawadet Labma, and Pol Staff Sgt Songsak Khomkong; BPP Regiment 11 (กก.ตชด.11), led by Pol Col Chariphat Thongdaeng; Rapid Deployment Force (นปพ.1); Thung Benchha Police Station).

Title: Authorities Seize Pistol During Casino Raid in Samut Prakan
Location: 4th Floor, Chaloem Wong Hotel, Soi Suk Sawat 80, Pak Khlong Bang Pla Kot Subdistrict, Phra Samut Chedi District, Samut Prakan
Description:
The authorities seized one military-grade firearm, one pistol, and ammunition during a raid on an illegal casino at the Chaloem Wong Hotel, Soi Suk Sawat 80, Phra Samut Chedi, Samut Prakan on 21 May 2026. The operation deployed over 70 officers from the Department of Provincial Administration special operations unit and Voluntary Defence Corps following tip-offs about a large gambling den running "Tua" and "Hi-Lo" games with table stakes of THB 700,000–1,000,000 per round and a live-streaming component.

Weapons were found at the hotel office desk and seized pending investigation of their link to casino security. Cash and chips worth between THB 5-8 million were also seized, along with financial ledgers showing daily turnover of THB 43 million linked to Prachaya Chaloem Wong ("Sia Boy"), a former Deputy Mayor of Lat Luang Municipality and suspected casino organizer. Between 50-63 people were arrested. Police noted the casino had only been operating for three days having relocated from Yaowarat, Bangkok.

Police

Title: Police Seize Glock Pistol After Vehicle Strikes Traffic Officer at Bangkok Checkpoint
Location: Bang Na-Trat Road (Outbound) Km 1, Bang Na Nuea Subdistrict, Bang Na District, Bangkok
Description:
Police seized one Glock 19 9mm pistol and 24 rounds of 9mm ammunition from a vehicle whose driver struck a traffic officer, causing serious injury, while approaching an alcohol checkpoint on Bang Na-Trat Road, Bang Na, Bangkok on 22 May 2026.

A white Mitsubishi Xpander registered in Ubon Ratchathani, driven by Thanabat, 20, with Akkhrawin, 20, as passenger, failed to stop at the checkpoint and struck Pol 2nd Lt Tharan Phiromchitcharoen, 59, Deputy Traffic Inspector of Bang Na Police Station, who was standing on the footpath approximately 100 meters before the checkpoint. Tharan was hospitalized in a coma with severe brain injury, a fractured cervical spine, and diffuse hemorrhage. Officers blocked the vehicle with a pickup; the occupants surrendered. A search of the spare-tire compartment yielded the Glock 19 and 24 rounds. Neither suspect tested positive for alcohol or drugs.

Thanabat initially denied knowing about the firearm but later acknowledged it, saying he was a competitive shooter and the gun was in process of registration transfer, purchased for THB 85,000. Akkhrawin admitted membership in the "Oreo Gang" and had five prior records for assault and illegal firearm possession. Thanabat was charged with unlicensed firearm possession, carrying a firearm in public, and reckless driving causing grievous bodily harm; Akkhrawin was charged with unlicensed firearm possession and carrying a firearm in public.
Actors: Youth Gang (Akkhrawin, 20 — "Oreo Gang" member, passenger, arrested; Thanabat, 20 — driver, arrested); Police (Bang Na Police Station, led by Pol Col Surapon Sukhaem; Pol Lt Phaibun Mala; Pol Lt Col Sekkasit Phae Loi; Pol Lt Col Somkhit Bampheanbun; Pol 2nd Lt Tharan Phiromchitcharoen, 59 — victim, seriously injured).

Title: Cyber Police Seize Pistol and Two Pen Guns from Repeat Offender in Bangkok
Location: Phloemsin 24/1 Road, Khlong Thanon Subdistrict, Sai Mai District, Bangkok
Description: Police seized one 9mm pistol, two pen guns, and 16 rounds of ammunition from Jirayut, 27, at his home in Khlong Thanon Subdistrict, Sai Mai, Bangkok on 20 May 2026, after detecting social media posts in which he displayed firearms. Officers from Cyber Crime Suppression Division 4 found all weapons in Jirayut's bedroom. Jirayut stated the 9mm pistol and its 15 rounds belonged to a friend who had left them with him, while both pen guns (chambered for .38 and .22 respectively) were his own, purchased for THB 2,000 approximately one month prior. He acknowledged three prior arrests for illegal firearm possession; the most recent, in mid-2025.
Weapons: 1 9mm pistol (serial number obliterated); 2 craft pen guns; 16 rounds of ammunition
Actors: Criminal Individual (Jirayut, 27 — repeat offender, arrested); Police (Cyber Crime Suppression Division 4 (กก.4 บก.สอท.5), led by Pol Lt Gen Surapon Prembutr (ผบช.สอท.) and Pol Maj Gen Sila Kanchanarak (ผบก.สอท.5); Pol Col Natthaphong Trong-thiang (ผกก.4 บก.สอท.5); Pol Lt Col Khamsan Samon (สว.กก.4 บก.สอท.5))

Title: Training Explosive Detonates and Injures Instructor at Phetchaburi Military Base
Location: Camp Phra Ram VI, Cha-am District, Phetchaburi
Description:
A training explosive detonated at the Camp Phra Ram VI field hospital, Cha-am District, Phetchaburi on 22 May 2026, seriously injuring one police instructor. The injured officer — Pol Sen Sgt Maj Saksit Phukingngoen, 51 — was preparing explosive ordnance equipment for a training exercise when the device detonated, causing severe lacerations to his hands and body, heavy bleeding, and a weakened pulse. Camp medical personnel administered advanced first aid before he was transferred urgently to Hua Hin Hospital and subsequently to the Police Hospital. Camp Phra Ram VI (1st Regiment, Special Training Brigade, Border Patrol Police Command) issued a statement noting that the injured officer was an outside instructor, not a camp staff member.

Images of the remaining UXO showed two V-40 grenades; three K75 grenades, four Mk2 grenades, three M26 grenades, two flash grenades, one smoke grenade, and several inert/training grenades.
Actors: Police (Pol Sen Sgt Maj Saksit Phukingngoen, 51 — victim, EOD instructor; Cha-am Police Station, led by Pol Maj Saiyon Kheunnok Khum and Pol 2nd Lt Sakda Si Muang); Military (Camp Phra Ram VI / กก.1 บก.กฝ.บช.ตชด. — Border Patrol Police Command)

Location: Moo 1, Chakwa Subdistrict, Raman District, Yala / Suwari Subdistrict, Ruesoh District, Narathiwat border
Description: Presumed southern insurgents on a motorcycle captured a 9mm Norinco pistol when they shot and killed Isma-ae Maseela, 55, a former member of Special Task Force Regiment 47, along a road in Moo 1, Chakwa Subdistrict, Raman District, Yala on 22 May 2026. Isma-ae was riding a motorcycle toward his wife's home when unknown assailants on a motorcycle followed him and fired multiple rounds at close range, striking him five times in the head and neck and killing him instantly. The attackers then took his 9mm Norinco pistol before fleeing. Police and rangers attended the scene; forensic teams collected casings.

Location: Moo 5, Phanom Wang Subdistrict, Khuan Khanun District, Phatthalung
Description: Police seized one .45-calibre craft pistol and 24 rounds of ammunition along with 4,000 methamphetamine pills when they arrested Chaiwat Niam-mai, 25, of Moo 5, Phanom Wang Subdistrict, Khuan Khanun, Phatthalung on 23 May 2026.
