Title: Police Seize Firearm During Arrest of Indian National in Surat Thani
Location: Moo 1, Koh Phangan Subdistrict, Koh Phangan District, Surat Thani
Description: Police seized one firearm when they arrested Aphidet Khong-man alongside an Indian national, Shabeelkunnon Valappil ("Shabeel"), 33, in Moo 1, Koh Phangan Subdistrict, Koh Phangan District, Surat Thani on 23 May 2026. The primary arrest targeted Shabeel, a visa overstayer whose social media posts showing him handling long and short firearms and conducting target practice had been reported by local residents. Officers from Region 8 Criminal Suppression Division ambushed Shabeel as he returned to the property on a dirt bike. Upon arrest, drug paraphernalia was found concealed in his clothing. An examination of his phone revealed a drug menu with pricing; he was also linked to drug sales on Koh Samui. Aphidet Khong-man was separately arrested at the scene and found in possession of one firearm and drugs. Shabeel was charged with overstaying, unlicensed possession of Class 1 narcotics (methamphetamine ice, MDMA) and Class 2 narcotics (ketamine) for supply, and drug use.
Actors: Criminal Individual (Shabeelkunnon Valappil/"Shabeel", 33 — Indian national, visa overstayer, drug dealer); Police (Region 8 Criminal Suppression Division (กก.ปพ.บก.สส.ภ.8), led by Pol Gen Samran Nuanma (รอง ผบ.ตร./ผอ.ศปชก.ตร.), Pol Lt Gen Noppasin Phunlasawat, Pol Lt Gen Sittichai Lo-kankhai (ผบช.ภ.8), Pol Col Prawit Engchan (ผกก.ปพ.บก.สส.ภ.8); Koh Phangan Police Station)

Title: Police Seize Two Firearms from Minors Purchasing Weapons Online in Bangkok
Location: Narathiwat Ratchanakarin Road junction (U-turn), Thung Wat Don Subdistrict, Sathorn District, Bangkok
Description: Police seized one .38-calibre revolver, one craft handgun, and eight rounds of ammunition when patrol officers stopped two minors — Boy A, 16, and Boy B, 15 — who were collecting firearms purchased online near a road median in Sathorn, Bangkok on 24 May 2026. Officers observed Boy A hurriedly emerging from roadside shrubbery near a parked motorcycle while Boy B stood on the median; both displayed suspicious behavior. A search of their backpack yielded one silver .38 revolver in a leather holster with three rounds of ammunition, one craft short-barreled shotgun, and five rounds of 12-gauge ammunition. The boys stated they had paid THB 5,000 via LINE to an account using the name "Teacher One, Firearms Instructor" and had collected the weapons at the scene, saying they intended to use them for self-defense. Both were charged with joint unlicensed possession of firearms and ammunition and joint carrying of firearms in a public place without authorisation, and were transferred to Yannawa Police Station.
Weapons: 1 .38-calibre revolver; 1 craft short-barrelled shotgun; 3 rounds of .38 ammunition; 5 rounds of 12-gauge ammunition
Actors: Criminal individual (online arms seller, unidentified); Youth Gang (Boy A, 16; Boy B, 15 — both arrested); Police (Yannawa Police Station Patrol Unit 2; Pol Lt Gen Siam Bunyasom (ผบช.น.); Pol Maj Gen Chaikkrit Phoa (ผบก.น.6); Pol Col Phansa Amapha-phithak (ผกก.สน.ยานนาวา))

Title: Border Patrol Police Seize Handgun From Drug Trafficker in Chiang Rai
Location: Soi 5, Mae Kee Village, Moo 9, Pa Sang Subdistrict, Mae Chan District, Chiang Rai
Description: Border Patrol Police shot and killed an armed drug courier — Arko, 25 — and seized approximately 1,130,000 yaba tablets and one .38-calibre pistol during a vehicle pursuit in Pa Sang Subdistrict, Mae Chan District, Chiang Rai on 24 May 2026. Intelligence indicated a large drug shipment was being transported from the Thai-Myanmar border area near Mae Fah Luang District; BPP Company 327 officers identified a suspicious grey-silver four-door Isuzu pickup (Phitsanulok plates). After the driver — Arko — eluded initial contact, the vehicle ran off the road at a rice paddy. Arko fled on foot into the field and opened fire on pursuing officers with the .38 pistol, approximately 100 meters from the vehicle; officers returned fire and killed him. The vehicle's flatbed contained 6 sacks disguised as grass bales and grass jelly drink boxes, holding approximately 1,130,000 yaba tablets stamped "999" in blue.
Actors: Criminal Organization (drug trafficking network — unidentified organizers); Criminal Individual (Arko, 25 — stateless drug courier, killed); Police (BPP Company 327 (ร้อย.ตชด.327), led by Pol Col Ratthakorn Inthanin (ผกก.ตชด.32), Pol Lt Col Chawalit Khamtue (รอง ผกก.ตชด.32), Pol Capt Thanawat Thipwaree (ผบ.ร้อย ตชด.327); BPP Region 3, led by Pol Maj Gen Woraphatt Bunma; Mae Chan Police Station)

Title: Police Seize Type 56-2 Rifles and Over 7,000 Rounds From Arms Traffickers near Cambodia
Location: Highway 24, between Ban Na Krasaeng and Ban Nong Ngoen Hoi, Na Krasaeng Subdistrict, Det Udom District, Ubon Ratchathani
Description:
Police seized five Type 56-2 assault rifles and 7,118 rounds of ammunition when they arrested a married couple — Surachet Kong-thong, 46, and Suphanasa Tri-bun, 39 — on Highway 24 in Det Udom District, Ubon Ratchathani on 25 May 2026. Acting on informant intelligence that a large illegal arms shipment would transit the area, Det Udom Police Station investigators coordinated with Ubon Ratchathani Provincial Police CID and Highway Police Unit 4 Group 6 to intercept the couple's grey Toyota Camry (Rayong plates).

When stopped at Ban Na Krasaeng, officers opened the boot and discovered the weapons hidden in fertilizer sacks. Surachet confessed that the arms belonged to "Wittaya," a close friend with whom he had served in Rayong Prison, now residing in a neighboring country. Wittaya hired Surachet at THB 15,000–20,000 per run to collect weapons at a petrol station in Prasat District, Surin Province (approx. 10 km north of the Thai-Cambodian border), and transport them by parcel service to Chiang Mai and Lamphun. This was their third delivery run.

Military sources assessed the weapons were smuggled in from Cambodia, consistent with a January 2026 case at Warin Chamrap, Ubon Ratchathani, in which AK ammunition was discovered hidden in Cambodian military clothing via a private courier. The couple were charged with joint possession of unlicensed firearms and ammunition without authorization, and with carrying them in a public place without lawful reason.
Weapons: 5 Type 56-2 assault rifles; 6,450 rounds of 7.62mm ammunition; 168-255 rounds of 12.7mm API ammunition; 500 rounds of M60 belt-fed ammunition (total 7,118 rounds)
Actors: Criminal organization (arms traffickers); Police (Det Udom Police Station (สภ.เดชอุดม); Ubon Ratchathani Provincial Police CID (กก.สส.ภ.จว.อุบลราชธานี); Highway Police Unit TL.4 Group 6 (ส.ทล.4 กก.6 บก.ทล.); Ubon Ratchathani Provincial Police (ภ.จว.อุบลราชธานี))

Title: Authorities Seize Pistol in Large Operation Against Drug Trafficking Network in Loei
Location: Mueang Loei District, Loei
Description:
Narcotics police, military intelligence, and US DEA seized approximately 20 million yaba tablets and one Glock 19 semi-automatic pistol with ammunition across a series of operations targeting an organized youth drug-trafficking network in Mueang Loei District, Loei between 23 and 25 May 2026. The network transported drugs from the northeastern border, staged them in rented warehouses in Loei, and relayed them to Bangkok and central Thailand using multiple vehicles and rotating teams to evade detection.

On 23 May, a joint team raided two staging sites — a warehouse at 163 Highway 21 (Loei–Phetchabun road), Moo 2, Ban Siao, and a safehouse at 1/35 Highway (Wangsapung–Phu Luang road), Kut Pong — and arrested four suspects, seizing approximately 10 million tablets at each location and six vehicles. On 19 May, personnel seized a Glock 19 pistol with ammunition and assets valued at over THB 5.5 million during a separate intelligence-led arrest targeting network leader Narasak ("Toei"), 28. In total across related cases, 11 incidents were recorded, with 33 suspects arrested, and combined seizures including over 15.5 million yaba tablets, approximately 4,800 kg of ice, and over 500 kg of ketamine.
Actor Type:
Criminal Organization
Police
Military
Other State Agency
Actors: Criminal Organization (drug trafficking network — Narasak/"Toei", 28 (arrested, commander); three other named suspects arrested in Loei operation); Police (Narcotics Suppression Bureau Division 1 Group 2 (ส่วนปราบปรามขยายผล กก.2 บก.ปส.1); Pol Gen Samran Nuanma (รอง ผบ.ตร./ผอ.ศอ.ปส.ตร.); Pol Lt Gen Achayan Kraithong (ผบช.ปส.); Pol Maj Gen Noppsit Mitraphakdi (ผบก.ปส.1); Transport Suppression Bureau (บก.ขส.); Loei Provincial Police Intelligence Unit); Military (Second Army Region (ทภ.2); Special Operations Command (นบ.ยส.24); กก.ควบคุมที่ 3 (ร.8)); Other State Agency (US Drug Enforcement Administration (DEA); ONCB Region 1 (ป.ป.ส. ภาค 1))

Title: Police Seize Converted Pistol from Drug Network in Ayutthaya
Location: Highway 33, Km 52+500, Bang Rakam Subdistrict, Nakhon Luang District, Phra Nakhon Si Ayutthaya
Description:
Police seized one modified pistol (blank gun) and 51 rounds of ammunition alongside approximately 6.8 million yaba tablets when they arrested four members of the "Nak Bin Chan Daen" (Border-Crossing Pilot) drug network at Highway 33, Km 52, Nakhon Luang, Ayutthaya on 26 May 2026. The network transported drugs from Phetchabun Province to a rented warehouse in Moo 1, Krachiu Subdistrict, Phachi District, Ayutthaya, awaiting redistribution to central Thailand and the south.

Officers staking out the warehouse observed four suspects load the drugs onto a sealed-body pickup truck and followed them until intercepting them on Highway 33, where they recovered approximately 1 million tablets from the vehicle. A follow-on search of the warehouse yielded another 5.8 million tablets. The four suspects — Thiraphhat ("Tui"), 24; Somchai ("Dong"), 35; Amnat ("Ak"), 35; and Nichada ("Nun"), 19 — were charged with joint supply of Class 1 narcotics for trade, possessing a firearm without authorisation, and carrying a firearm in a public place without authorisation.
Weapons: 1 modified .380-calibre pistol (converted blank gun); 51 rounds of ammunition
Actor Type:
Criminal Organization
Criminal Individual
Police
Other State Agency
Actors: Criminal Organization ("Nak Bin Chan Daen" drug network); Criminal Individual (Thiraphhat/"Tui", 24; Somchai/"Dong", 35; Amnat/"Ak", 35; Nichada/"Nun", 19 — all arrested); Police (Region 1 Provincial Police (ตำรวจภูธรภาค 1), led by Pol Lt Gen Watthana Yichain (ผบช.ภ.1); Phra Nakhon Si Ayutthaya Provincial Police (ภ.จว.พระนครศรีอยุธยา), led by Pol Maj Gen Surawut Sangrungrueng (ผบก.); Nakhon Luang Police Station (สภ.นครหลวง), led by Pol Col Srayut Saengthong (ผกก.); Phra Nakhon Si Ayutthaya CID); Other State Agency (ONCB Region 1 (ป.ป.ส. ภาค 1), led by Sasa-rat Nam-wong)

Title: Police Seize Revolver After Deadly Neighbor Dispute in Tak
Location: Village No. 1, Mae Lamung Subdistrict, Umphang District, Tak
Description: Police seized one .22-calibre revolver when Aphichat Jaiya, 32, shot and killed Siwakorn Wongkrot, 55, outside Siwakorn's home at 11 Moo 1, Mae Lamung Subdistrict, Umphang District, Tak on 26 May 2026. Following an argument that escalated, Aphichat used the .22 revolver he was carrying to shoot Siwakorn once in the head at close range, killing him instantly. After the incident, Aphichat did not flee and was found standing at the scene in a panicked state when Umphang Police Station officers arrived.
Actors: Criminal Individual (Aphichat Jaiya, 32 — suspected murderer); Police (Umphang Police Station (สภ.อุ้มผาง))

Title: Security Forces Seize Pistol from Wanted Insurgent in Yala Encounter
Location: Moo 3, Ban Lubo Panyang, Kabang Subdistrict, Kabang District, Yala
Description:
Security forces seized a CZ75 SP-01 pistol when they killed Saidi Thakunae, 33, a wanted insurgent suspect, during a law enforcement operation at Moo 3, Ban Lubo Panyang, Kabang Subdistrict, Kabang District, Yala on 26 May 2026 after negotiations to surrender failed.

Saidi, subject to an arrest warrant (Yala Provincial Court No. 347/2562, issued 21 December 2019) for terrorism, weapons accumulation, conspiracy, attempted murder of officials, and unlicensed possession of firearms, explosives, and war materiel, had been linked to multiple prior incidents including a bombing in Yala municipality (June 2015), a Krung Thai Bank ATM bombing at Ban Bannung Dama, Kabang (August–September 2025), and a PTT petrol station bombing at Bannung Dama (11 January 2026).

Security forces entered the property with community leaders attempting to facilitate a surrender; Saidi fired on officers, who returned fire and killed him. EOD and forensic teams (Forensic Science Center 10) subsequently processed the scene for weapons, casings, and other evidence. The homeowner, Sanusi (surname withheld), was brought in for questioning.
Actor Type:
NSAG
Military
Police
Other State Agency
Actors: NSAG (presumed BRN); Military (ISOC Region 4 Forward (กองอำนวยการรักษาความมั่นคงภายในภาค 4 ส่วนหน้า)); Police (forensic teams); Other State Agency (EOD unit)

Title: Police Seize Craft Handgun from 19-Year-Old in Bangkok
Location: Soi Ramintra 117, Intersection 16, Min Buri Subdistrict, Min Buri District, Bangkok
Description: Police seized one craft handgun and one round of ammunition when they arrested a 19-year-old Thai male near Nurul Islam (Khukot) Mosque, Soi Ramintra 117 Intersection 16, Min Buri District, Bangkok on 26 May 2026. Min Buri Police Station patrol officers were conducting routine crime prevention patrols in the area when they found the suspect in possession of the weapon. He was charged with unlicensed possession of a firearm and ammunition and carrying a firearm in a city, village, or public road without authorisation, and was transferred to Min Buri Police Station.
Actors: Police (Min Buri Police Station)

Title: Police Seize Craft Pistol from Drug-Affected Man in Sisaket
Location: Rattanawongsa Road, Mueang District, Sisaket
Description: Traffic police seized one .38-calibre craft (presumably converted) pistol and six rounds of ammunition from Danai, 21, after he attempted to draw the weapon on officers during a routine vehicle check on Rattanawongsa Road, Mueang Sisaket, on 26 May 2026. When officers signaled Danai to stop his motorcycle and he opened the seat compartment for inspection, they spotted the weapon. Danai lunged for the weapon; officers intervened and he fled on foot into the grounds of Sisaket Primary Educational Area Office 1, where a cordon quickly located him. He was in an agitated, incoherent state consistent with drug intoxication, and during transport repeatedly headbutted the police vehicle and bit his own tongue while shouting that he wanted to die. A further search of the motorcycle yielded four yaba tablets hidden in a cigarette packet under the seat.
Actors: Police (Mueang Sisaket Police Station)

Title: Police Seize Six Pistols and Illegal Craft Rifle from Occultist in Chiang Mai
Location: Moo 2, Nong Khwai Subdistrict, Hang Dong District, Chiang Mai
Description: Police seized six registered pistols and one illicit craft rifle from Kamolthep ("Ajarn Suea Ban Fon"), 43, a well-known occultist and tattoo master, when they arrested him on suspicion of rape at his home and studio in Moo 2, Nong Khwai Subdistrict, Hang Dong District, Chiang Mai on 26 May 2026. On inspecting the licensed firearms and the illegal craft rifle, officers also found a small amount of yaba tablets. Kamolthep denied the rape allegation, claiming he could not remember entering the venue, and stated the firearms were kept for his love of sport shooting and for ritual use when entering cemeteries to collect ceremonial items. He was charged with rape, unlicensed possession of a firearm (the craft rifle), and possession of narcotics. Police opposed bail. Kamolthep had a prior narcotics conviction in 2008.
Weapons: 6 pistols (licensed); 1 craft rifle (illegal); multiple rounds of ammunition (quantities not specified in sources)
Actors: Criminal Individual (Kamolthep Chaisri/"Ajarn Suea Ban Fon", 43 — occultist, arrested); Police (Hang Dong Police Station)

Title: Authorities Seize Craft Pistol from Two Youths in Mae Sot
Location: Mae Sot District, Tak
Description: Administrative authorities seized one converted Glock-pattern pistol when they arrested two youths in Mae Sot District, Tak on 27 May 2026. The operation followed community complaints about the individuals' behavior, including gathering, loud motorcycle riding, and suspected drug-related activities. A search of their residence turned up a Glock-pattern pistol along with narcotics (white powder in test tubes and cannabis) and drug paraphernalia.
Actors: Other State Agency (Mae Sot District Administrative Office)

Title: Authorities Seize Two Pistols from Monk After Social Media Post in Phang Nga
Location: Wat Yan Yao, Takua Pa Subdistrict (then 56/10 Moo 2, Bang Nai Si Subdistrict), Takua Pa District, Phang Nga
Description: Administrative authorities seized one .32-calibre semi-automatic pistol and one Trailblazer Firearms LifeCard .22 WMR folding pistol when they inspected a monk — Jessada ("Phra Chom"), 21 — who had posted a video showing him cleaning a firearm at Wat Yan Yao, Takua Pa District, Phang Nga on 27 May 2026. The suspect tested positive for methamphetamine. Jessada acknowledged keeping the firearms at his home at 56/10 Moo 2, Bang Nai Si Subdistrict; a search there found both pistols along with one .32-calibre round hidden in his bedroom. He was charged with unlicensed possession of firearms and ammunition and methamphetamine use. The abbot immediately defrocked him.
Weapons: 1 .32-calibre semi-automatic pistol; 1 Trailblazer Firearms LifeCard .22 WMR folding pistol; 1 round of .32-calibre ammunition
Actors: Other State Agency (Takua Pa District Office, led by District Chief Damrong Chimtap; District Security Officer Aphisit Thong-kammak; Or Sor Takua Pa Company 7 (ร้อย อส.อ.ตะกั่วป่า ที่ 7))

Title: Police Seize .38 Pistol and Shotgun Following Domestic Disturbance in Udon Thani
Location: Mueang Udon Thani District, Udon Thani
Description: Police seized one .38-calibre pistol and one shotgun when they arrested Tam, 38, after his stepmother — Chatchada, 60 — reported him for repeatedly firing a gun outside their home in Mueang Udon Thani on 27 May 2026. Tam, the son of a recently deceased police officer, had used inheritance money (approximately THB 300,000 from a police benefit fund) to purchase the firearms and yaba tablets. He admitted firing shots to vent frustration but claimed his volatile temperament stemmed from a dog bite to his face at age eight that required 138 stitches.
Actors: Police (Mueang Udon Thani Police Station, led by Pol Col Phatthanawong Chanphon (ผกก.); Pol Maj Chakraphong Tong-taem (สว.สอบสวน))

Title: Police Seize Sig Sauer Pistol from Drug Dealer Couple in Bangkok
Location: 89/25 Nirand Residence 5, Prawet Subdistrict, Prawet District, Bangkok
Description:
Police seized one Sig Sauer pistol and seven rounds of ammunition when they arrested a married couple — Wiwatthani Chantharangsri, 33, and Phongsathorn Semsam-ran, 38 — outside their apartment in Prawet District, Bangkok on 28 May 2026. Officers had been targeting the couple's Facebook page "Baifern Wiwan" for illegally selling e-cigarettes to minors; a decoy purchase led investigators to the Nirand Residence 5 address.

While officers were detaining Wiwatthani at the front of the unit, Phongsathorn emerged from inside, visibly alarmed, then re-entered and returned carrying a bag containing 117 yaba tablets (marked "WY"), 2 grams of crystal meth, and the weapon. He stated he voluntarily handed over the items to demonstrate good faith, fearing a heavier sentence if the items were found inside the apartment during a search.
Weapons: 1 Sig Sauer pistol; 7 rounds of ammunition
Actors: Criminal Individual (Phongsathorn Semsam-ran, 38 — drug dealer/suspect, surrendered; Wiwatthani Chantharangsri, 33 — e-cigarette seller, arrested); Police (Consumer Protection Police Division 1 (กก.1 บก.ปคบ.), led by Pol Maj Gen Khongkrit Loet-sitthikul (ผบก.ปคบ.), Pol Col Kraiwit Saen-thawisuk (ผกก.1 บก.ปคบ.), and Pol Maj Krit Phiphat-phunsiri (สว.กก.1 บก.ปคบ.))

Title: Hunter Accidentally Shoots and Kills Friend While Poaching in Chiang Rai
Location: Huay Nam Haai stream area (Ban Rong, Moo 2), Mae Chedi Mai Subdistrict, Wiang Pa Pao District, Chiang Rai
Description: Police seized one unregistered shotgun when Bunchoet, 54, accidentally shot and killed his fellow hunter Wirachat Khuen-sati, 46, while poaching in a forest approximately 2.5 km from Ban Rong village, Wiang Pa Pao District, Chiang Rai on 28 May 2026. The two had entered the Huay Nam Haai forest separately. In the darkness, Bunchoet shone a torch and saw two eyes in the undergrowth, mistaking Wirachat for a wild boar, and fired one round. He found Wirachat shot through the torso. Bunchoet called Wirachat's wife, then notified local officials and police. The unregistered shotgun was seized as evidence. Wirachat was taken to Wiang Pa Pao Hospital and subsequently transferred to Chiang Rai Prachanukroh Hospital. Bunchoet was charged with negligently causing death.
Actors: Criminal Individual (suspected poachers); Police (Mae Chedi Police Station (สภ.แม่เจดีย์), led by Pol Col Suphap Khuean-kaeo (ผกก.) and Pol 2nd Lt Sahatwat Uad-lap (รอง สว.สอบสวน))

Title: Police Seize Sig Sauer P320 from E-Cigarette Dealing Brothers in Bangkok
Location: 235 Ngampanja 2 Community, Soi Bang Khae 9, Bang Khae Subdistrict, Bang Khae District, Bangkok
Description: Police seized one Sig Sauer P320 SP 9mm pistol and 15 rounds of ammunition when they arrested two brothers for e-cigarette distribution at 235 Ngampanja 2 Community, Bang Khae, Bangkok on 29 May 2026. The investigation had established that the two brothers were distributing illegal vaping products and yaba to youths in the community. Officers arrested Natthaphong ("Oat"), 29, for possession and distribution of prohibited e-cigarette products (RELX e-cigarette bodies and pod heads), and his elder brother Watcharaphong ("Mark"), 32, for unlicensed possession of a firearm, ammunition, and two yaba tablets. Both were transferred to Phetchakasem Police Station.
Weapons: 1 Sig Sauer P320 SP 9mm pistol; 15 rounds of ammunition
Actors: Criminal Individual (Natthaphong/"Oat", 29 — e-cigarette dealer, arrested; Watcharaphong/"Mark", 32 — firearms and drug offences, arrested); Police (Phetchakasem Police Station (สน.เพชรเกษม), led by Pol Col Pramot Chanbunkae (ผกก.); Pol Maj Worangkron Khajonbunyawat (รอง ผกก.สส.); Pol Maj Thawatchai Thipwong (สว.สส.))

Title: Official Suspended Amid Revelation of Improper Firearms Licenses and Online Gambling Links in Narathiwat
Location: Suai Go-lok (Sungai Kolok) District, Narathiwat
Description:
A Ministry of Interior disciplinary memorandum dated 7 July 2025 was leaked to multiple Thai media outlets on 23 May 2026, revealing that Rungrueang Thimabut, former Deputy Governor of Phuket, had improperly issued 26 firearms licenses (Por.4) in Suai Go-lok District, Narathiwat during his tenure as district chief.

The disclosure coincided with an emergency transfer order issued on 20 May 2026 under which Rungrueang and four other Phuket provincial administration officials were reassigned to Bangkok on temporary duty (DOPA Order No. 1512/2569, effective 25 May–24 June 2026) to prevent evidence tampering during an ongoing investigation into bribe-taking from entertainment venues in Patong.

The leaked memorandum, signed by former DOPA Director-General Nirat Phongsittithawin (now Phuket Governor), detailed two serious allegations: first, Rungrueang had issued Por.4 purchase and possession licenses for 26 firearms to one Sam Waedao without conducting required background checks, income verification, or character references — Sam Waedao was subsequently found to have drug-trafficking links; second, financial trail evidence linked bank accounts and a mobile phone registered to Rungrueang to transactions totaling tens of millions of baht connected to an online gambling network operated by Hariph Kodae, whose SCB account transferred at least THB 16 million to five accounts in Rungrueang's name across 334 transactions between January 2021 and October 2022. The committee sanctioned Rungrueang with a 2% salary cut for three months. A separate criminal warrant from the Bangkok South Criminal Court had been issued against him for co-facilitating and advertising unlicensed gambling and money laundering.

Rungrueang responded publicly on 24 May 2026, questioning the timing of the leak and describing it as an attempt to discredit him.
Weapons: 26 firearms of unspecified types (shotguns, pistols — exact make/model not reported); licensed under Por.4 forms issued to Sam Waedao without proper checks. No weapons seized in this cluster.
Actor Type:
Civilian Official
Criminal Individual
Criminal Organization
Other State Agency
Civilian Community
Actors: Civilian Official (Rungrueang Thimabut — former Nai Amphoe Suai Go-lok, Deputy Governor of Phuket, suspended/transferred; sanctioned for non-severe disciplinary violation); Criminal Individual (Sam Waedao — recipient of 26 improperly issued firearms licences, subsequently found to have drug-trafficking links); Criminal Organization (online gambling network linked via financial transactions to accounts associated with Rungrueang); Other State Agency (Department of Provincial Administration / DOPA — DG Narucha Khosasiwilaich, former DG Nirat Phongsittithawin; AMLO — financial trail investigation); Civilian Community (Patong Beach entertainment venue operators — complainants)

Title: DOPA Operation Dismantles Cross-Border Arms Trafficking Network in Chiang Khong, Seizing Three Long Guns and Over 400 Forged Firearms Purchase Licences
Location: Chiang Khong District, Chiang Rai (primary); Phrom Phiram District, Phitsanulok (follow-on)
Description:
Department of Provincial Administration (DOPA) special operations personnel seized three rifles, over 200 rounds of ammunition, and more than 400 forged firearms purchase license (Por.3) documents when they arrested four suspects during a coordinated operation across Chiang Khong District, Chiang Rai and Phitsanulok on 29 May 2026, dismantling a structured cross-border arms trafficking network.

The investigation originated from DOPA's detection of anomalies in Chiang Khong District's firearms licensing database during 2022–2023: large numbers of Por.3 purchase authorizations had been issued but the corresponding Por.4 ownership licenses were never registered, indicating the weapons were not retained by the nominally licensed individuals. The network's method was to forge official signatures and documentation — drawing on the identities of village headmen, assistant headmen, health volunteers, community defense volunteers, and sub-district chiefs without their knowledge — to purchase firearms through official channels for onward export to neighboring countries (presumed Laos or Myanmar).

At Chiang Khong, officers arrested Natthawhut Watthanaphanon, a Volunteer Defense Corps (Or Sor) member responsible for collecting applicant names and documentation, at his residence. The principal organizer — former sub-district chief Aphisit Jankham, 42, dismissed from the civil service for impersonating a foreign national at Wiang Haeng District — was not found and remained at large. Three rifles, over 200 rounds, and 400+ forged Por.3 documents were seized. Each Por.3 document corresponded to one firearm, indicating at least 400 weapons channeled through the scheme.

In the Phitsanulok follow-on operation, a team directed by the Phitsanulok Governor arrested the owner of the 'Puen Mor' gun shop on Ramessuan Road and three associates, transferring all four to Chiang Khong Police Station. DOPA Director-General Narucha characterized the network as having four distinct tiers: financiers, brokers handling weapons for export, corrupt officials forging documents, and participating licensed gun shops.
Weapons: 3 rifles; over 200 rounds of ammunition; 400+ forged Por.3 firearms purchase licence documents (corresponding to approx. 400 diverted firearms of multiple types including shotguns, .22 rifles, pistols, and revolvers)
Actor Type:
Criminal Organization
Criminal Individual
State Auxiliary
Other State Agency
Police
Actors: Criminal Organization (cross-border arms trafficking network — financiers, brokers, corrupt officials, gun shop participants; weapons diverted to neighbouring countries); Criminal Individual (Aphisit Jankham, 42 — former sub-district chief, dismissed civil servant, principal organizer, at large; Natthawhut Watthanaphanon — Or Sor member, arrested; Phitsanulok gun dealer "Puen Mor" and three associates, arrested); State Auxiliary (Volunteer Defence Corps / Or Sor — Natthawhut, participant); Other State Agency (DOPA / กรมการปกครอง — DG Narucha Khosasiwilaich, Deputy DGs Ronnorong Thipsiri and Wiroon Sitthiwong, Pol Lt Col Singkham Khamyot, Sakdachai Rotjanarat; Phitsanulok Provincial Administration; Interior Ministry Permanent Secretary Arrasit Samphanrat); Police (Chiang Khong Police Station; Anti-Corruption Criminal Court Region 5 — warrant authority)

Title: Insurgents Use Three Diverted Firearms to Shoot Police Officer's Family in Pattani
Location: Road leading to Prasanwitthaya Foundation School, Moo 5, Pong Sata Subdistrict, Yarung District, Pattani
Description:
Presumed insurgents used three government-issue firearms — an AK-pattern assault rifle and two Glock pistols, all previously diverted from security forces — when they ambushed the vehicle of Police Sergeant Adul Haji Sulong, as he drove to collect his children from school in Pong Sata Subdistrict, Yarung District, Pattani on 25 May 2026. Four attackers disguised as Muslim women in hijabs arrived on two motorcycles and opened fire on Adul's vehicle as it entered the narrow lane leading to the school. Adul's wife, Patimoh Yago, 35, was killed in the shooting.

Forensic investigators analyzed the 11 spent cartridges and confirmed three weapons were used. Weapon 1, an AK-pattern rifle, had previously been used in two confirmed prior attacks in June 2024 and February 2025. The February 2025 ambush at Yarung Police Station killed two police personnel and resulted in the theft of their service firearms. Weapon 2, a Glock 9mm pistol (serial RTP6744), was stolen from Pol Const Songchai Chantaraphap in that same February 2025 ambush. Weapon 3, a second Glock 9mm, had previously been used in a January 2023 incident in Thepha District, Songkhla. A loaded magazine with 10 rounds was found at the scene.
Weapons: 1 AK-pattern assault rifle (government-issue, diverted); 1 Glock 9mm pistol (government-issue, serial RTP6744, stolen Feb 2025); 1 Glock 9mm pistol (government-issue, diverted). 10 rounds of 9mm ammunition (in abandoned magazine).
Actors: NSAG (presumed BRN); Police (Pol Sgt Adul Haji Sulong).

Description: Authorities seized one .38-caliber craft pistol and two rounds of ammunition along with 400 methamphetamine tablets and 46.08 grams of crystal meth when they raided the home of two suspects — Rangsan, 35, and Yasiri, 41 — in Phai Khwang Subdistrict, Mueang Suphan Buri, Suphan Buri on 29 May 2026. Title: Police Seize Pistol from Drug-Intoxicated Gunrunner at Checkpoint in Kanchanaburi
Location: Highway 367 (Bypass Road), Moo 1, Tha Makham Subdistrict, Mueang Kanchanaburi District, Kanchanaburi
Description: Traffic police seized one 7.65mm Type 54 pistol and two rounds of .32 ammunition along with six methamphetamine tablets when they stopped a Toyota pickup truck driven by Sitthiphon ('Nui'), 34, at a checkpoint in Tha Makham Subdistrict, Mueang Kanchanaburi, Kanchanaburi on 30 May 2026. Upon stopping the vehicle, officers observed suspicious behavior consistent with drug intoxication. A search found six yaba tablets under the driver's seat along with drug paraphernalia, and the pistol concealed in the center console with two rounds. The suspect admitted to purchasing seven yaba tablets for THB 200, consuming one before driving, and confirmed he was transporting the pistol for transfer to a buyer — he was arrested before completing the delivery.
Actors: Criminal Individual (Sitthiphon/'Nui', 34 — drug user and illicit arms courier, arrested); Police (Mueang Kanchanaburi Police Station).

Title: Police Seize Flint-Lock Rifle Following Deadly Rubber Plantation Dispute in Udon Thani
Location: Rubber plantation, Moo 9, Nong Waeng Subdistrict, Nam Som District, Udon Thani
Description: Police seized one craft flint-lock rifle when they arrested the son of a rubber plantation owner, after he shot and killed a woman at the plantation in Nong Waeng Subdistrict, Nam Som District, Udon Thani on 30 May 2026. Police found the victim's body beside a charcoal kiln east of Ban Somsaard and detained the suspect with the weapon approximately two kilometers from the scene. A dispute over latex theft reportedly motivated the murder.
Actors: Criminal Individual (Wisan, 38 — rubber plantation worker, arrested, charged with murder); Police (Nam Som Police Station, led by Pol Col Sarayut Chumpiu; Pol Maj Udon Yotpon; Pol 2nd Lt Phirayut Uchin).

Title: Police Seize Converted Pistol from Chinese-Thai Couple Running Illicit Vape Ring in Chiang Mai
Location: 175/3 Moo 10, Tha Wang Tan Subdistrict, Saraphi District, Chiang Mai
Description:
Police seized one converted .380-calibre handgun and 30 rounds of ammunition from a Chinese-Thai couple — Mr. Jianguo Gu and Ms. Ratchanee — at their luxury home in Tha Wang Tan Subdistrict, Saraphi District, Chiang Mai on 1 June 2026, as part of an operation dismantling a network producing and distributing etomidate-laced e-cigarette pods ('Port K') to entertainment venues.

Acting on informant intelligence, a search of the bedroom yielded etomidate in dozens of e-vape pod heads, production equipment, the modified Kimber-brand .380 BB gun with 30 rounds, and multiple mobile phones. Ratchanee stated she had purchased the modified pistol via Facebook for THB 20,000 for self-protection. A second property in Saraphi District was used as the production facility, where Mr. Cheng Chen was also arrested with 2.9 grams of crystal meth. The couple confessed to sourcing etomidate from a Myanmar national via Telegram, collecting it in Mae Sai, Chiang Rai, then mixing it into e-vape pods sold for THB 1,000–10,000 per unit at Chiang Mai entertainment venues targeting Chinese tourists. The operation had been running for approximately two months.
Actor Type:
Criminal Organization
Police
Other State Agency
Civilian Official

Title: Police Seize Craft Pistol From Youth Gang Members in Nonthaburi
Location: Rattanathibet Road (between Nambinnam Airport junction and PTT Bangchak petrol station), Mueang Nonthaburi District, Nonthaburi
Description: Police seized a craft .38 pistol and an air pistol when they arrested two 15-year-old members of the 'Sai Thong' gang on Rattanathibet Road, Mueang Nonthaburi, Nonthaburi on 1 June 2026. The arrest took place after the suspects opened fire on a 19-year-old rival on 29 May. The two offenders, named in sources as Mit and Pond, rode with a group of approximately ten friends when they came across the victim, 'Bank', 19, and his group. After the confrontation, Mit and Pond pulled alongside Bank's motorcycle and fired approximately 8–9 shots; Bank fled by riding the wrong way on the road and was uninjured. Mit stated the shooting was provoked by an obscene gesture from the other group and that he had committed similar incidents two or three times previously.
Actors: Youth Gang (Mit, 15, and Pond, 15 — both members of 'Sai Thong' gang, arrested); Police (Rattanathibet Police Station, led by Pol Col Phrot Chamrunsart; Pol Maj Phusinton Yiphu; Pol Maj Pracha Hungwonweerakun).

Title: Murder Suspect Surrenders Revolver Following Deadly Dispute with Sister-in-Law in Chonburi
Location: Soi 22 Ban Kao, Moo 3, Ban Kao Subdistrict, Phan Thong District, Chonburi
Description: A man surrendered one .38-calibre revolver and four rounds of ammunition to police after he shot and killed his sister-in-law at a rental property in Ban Kao, Phan Thong District, Chonburi on 1 June 2026. Police recovered the victim's body where she had fallen outside the rental rooms. Chana told officers he was the brother of the victim's husband and had long-standing disputes with his sister-in-law over land. The confrontation escalated over a coffee stand and septic tank the victim had placed near the rental rooms. After an argument the previous day over banana tree cuttings encroaching on his land, the disputes came to a head when he walked over and challenged her. His account describes a struggle over the pistol before it discharged once. Police charged him with intentional homicide, and carrying a firearm in a public place.
Weapons: 1 .38-calibre revolver (5 rounds loaded, 1 fired, 4 remaining at surrender).
Actors: Criminal Individual (Chana/'Pong', 75 — perpetrator, self-surrendered, charged); Police (Phan Thong Police Station).

Title: EOD Recovers Live MK 2 Grenade from Rubbish Pile Outside Bank in Bangkok
Location: Bang Khun Non Road, Bang Khun Non Subdistrict, Bangkok Noi District, Bangkok
Description: EOD personnel recovered an active MK 2 hand grenade from a trash collection point in front of Krungthai Bank's Bang Khun Non branch, Bang Khun Non Road, Bangkok Noi, Bangkok on 2 June 2026. Municipal workers discovered the item when they were sorting waste and immediately notified police. Police assess it was likely abandoned by someone seeking to avoid legal consequences for possessing it.
Actors: Police (Bang Khun Non Police Station; EOD unit).

Title: Authorities Seize Firearm and 160,000 Yaba Tablets from Drug Courier in Krabi
Location: Phetkasem Road, Thap Prik Subdistrict, Mueang Krabi District, Krabi
Description: Authorities seized one firearm, approximately 160,000 methamphetamine tablets, and an unspecified quantity of crystal meth after pursuing and stopping a white Mitsubishi pickup truck driven by a suspect on Phetkasem Road outbound, Thap Prik Subdistrict, Mueang Krabi on 4 June 2026. Officers had arranged for an informant to conduct a controlled purchase of over 160,000 yaba tablets from a member of the 'Bo Pla Gat Hua Kua' trafficking network based in Trang Province, with delivery in Krabi. When 'A' arrived at the handover point and sensed the operation, he attempted to flee in the pickup; officers pursued and shot out the vehicle's tires, causing it to stop. The suspect was arrested and the firearms and narcotics were seized. The trafficking network was reported to receive orders from abroad.
Actors: Criminal Organization ('Bo Pla Gat Hua Kua' drug trafficking network; 'A', pseudonym — arrested); Other State Agency (Mueang Krabi District Special Operations Unit, ordered by Preecha Soingthong).

Title: Security Forces Seize Handguns from Suspected BRN Insurgents in Pattani Shootout
Location: Moo 10, Tha Klong Village, Khok Pho Subdistrict, Khok Pho District, Pattani
Description:
Security forces seized one Sig Sauer P365 9mm pistol, one Smith & Wesson .38-calibre revolver, and one improvised pipe bomb when they killed two suspected BRN insurgents at a residence in Tha Klong Village, Moo 10, Khok Pho Subdistrict, Khok Pho District, Pattani on 4 June 2026. One security personnel sustained minor injuries.

Over 40 security personnel surrounded the property following intelligence indicated the suspects were hiding there. The homeowner and five family members exited and confirmed the suspects were inside. Negotiations and appeals by community leaders failed; the suspects opened fire, initiating an exchange as the suspects attempted to shoot their way out. The deceased were identified as Muslim Che-moh, 26, of Pata Subdistrict, Yaha District, Yala — wanted for the murder of a former ranger in Khok Pho in December 2023 — and Ro-asmi Do-loh, 28, of Pakaha-rang Subdistrict, Mueang Pattani — wanted for the April 2025 shooting of police officers and Buddhist monks/novices in Saba Yoi District, Songkhla, and for a pipe bomb attack near 15th Infantry Division in Nong Chik, Pattani in October 2023.
Weapons: 1 Sig Sauer P365 9mm pistol; 1 Smith & Wesson .38-calibre revolver.
Actors: NSAG (presumed BRN insurgents); Military (Special Task Force Regiment 43, ISOC Region 4 Forward); Police (Khok Pho Police Station).

Title: Police Seize Revolver and Drugs from Fugitive Drug Trafficker Hiding in Khon Kaen
Location: Resort, Ban Fang Subdistrict, Ban Fang District, Khon Kaen
Description: Police seized one .38-calibre revolver and 27 rounds of ammunition along with 38 methamphetamine tablets, 10.4 grams of crystal meth, and 53 packets of a psychoactive substance when they arrested five drug traffickers at a resort in Ban Fang Subdistrict, Ban Fang District, Khon Kaen on 5 June 2026. The main suspect, Phongkorn, 39, was a wanted drug trafficking network leader from Loei Province who had fled to Khon Kaen.
Actors: Criminal Organization (drug trafficking network from Loei); Police (CID Region 4 Group 1; Na Wang Police Station; Ban Fang Police Station).

Title: Police Seize Pistols, Shotgun, Rifle After Deadly Domestic Dispute in Bangkok
Location: 147 Soi Sakae-ngam 35/3, Samae Dam Subdistrict, Bang Khun Thian District, Bangkok
Description:
Police seized four firearms — two Glock 19 9mm pistols, one 12-gauge shotgun, and one .22-calibre rifle — following a domestic confrontation that left one dead at a shophouse in Samae Dam Subdistrict, Bang Khun Thian, Bangkok on 6 June 2026. Anirut Chim-dam, 50, intoxicated and agitated by a property dispute, forced his way into his nephew's bedroom carrying the weapons and threatened to shoot the family. His nephew Phanit Sribanyen, 20, grabbed one of the Glock 19s from the bed and shot Anirut four to five times; Anirut died at the scene. Phanit remained at the scene and surrendered the pistol to police.

Police found Anirut's body on the second-floor landing, shot multiple times with an empty Glock 19 (slide locked back, casings scattered) under his head. Police recovered the other three weapons on the bed in the room. Phanit told investigators the uncle had challenged his father to a duel, saying every gun was loaded and two shots each would kill everyone in the room. When his grandmother and mother failed to stop Anirut, Phanit grabbed the available Glock and fired. Sources note all four registered firearms were in the name of an ex-girlfriend who worked in the Nong Khaem District Office civil administration.
Actors: Criminal Individual (Anirut Chim-dam, 50 — perpetrator, deceased; Phanit Sribanyen, 20 — nephew, surrendered, charged); Police (Tha Kham Police Station); Civilian Official (deceased's ex-partner who originally owned the firearms).

Title: Police Seize Two Pistols After Deadly Durian Orchard Shootout in Chanthaburi
Location: Durian orchard ('Suan Dr. Dao'), Moo 7, Thung Bencha Subdistrict, Tha Mai District, Chanthaburi
Description:
Police seized a 9mm Taurus pistol and a Glock pistol from two vehicles after a durian-cutting crew and the orchard's owner exchanged gunfire at a fruit orchard in Thung Bencha Subdistrict, Tha Mai District, Chanthaburi on 6 June 2026, leaving the owner dead and several others wounded.

The clash erupted between Jittiphat 'Nop' Osatchareon, 51, the orchard owner, and a durian-buying crew led by Yutthana Sikhao, 49, after a dispute over delayed and mishandled harvesting escalated into a confrontation witnessed by roughly twenty workers from both sides. Jittiphat was shot in the head and died after being transferred to Phra Pokklao Hospital; his son or stepson, 21, and Yutthana's wife were also wounded, along with a third crew member. Responding officers found a blood trail and spent shell casings around the orchard's packing shed. Police searched the two pickup trucks that had carried the wounded to hospital, recovering a 9mm Taurus pistol from the buying crew's truck and a Glock pistol from the owner's truck, both sent for forensic testing alongside witness video footage.
Actors: Police (Thung Bencha Police Station, led by Pol Col Monthon Sangwai).

Title: Officers Seize Craft Pistol and Yaba Tablets From Drug Courier in Ubon Ratchathani
Location: Ban Bung Suay, Moo 2, Khemarat Subdistrict, Khemarat District, Ubon Ratchathani
Description:
Officers seized one craft 9mm pistol and six rounds of ammunition, along with approximately 728,000 methamphetamine tablets packed in four sacks, when they intercepted a pickup truck smuggling drugs across the Mekong River border in Ban Bung Suay, Khemarat District, Ubon Ratchathani on 6 June 2026. A joint team from Khemarat Police Station, Immigration Police, an Army task force, and a river security unit acted on a tip that a large shipment would move through the area; when the suspect vehicle arrived, the driver and a passenger fled on foot, but officers caught the driver, identified as Chakkri (surname withheld), 35, of Trakan Phuet Phon District.

Chakkri admitted he had been hired for THB 10,000 to drive the shipment, that this was his first job, and that he had not yet been paid before his arrest. He said the passenger who fled had handled coordination with contacts across the border in Laos.

Description: Police seized one craft rifle and a single methamphetamine tablet when they arrested an unidentified suspect at an unnumbered field shelter in Wang Yang Subdistrict, Phanna Nikhom District, Sakon Nakhon on 7 June 2026.
Actors: Criminal Individual (unidentified suspect, arrested); Police (Phanna Nikhom Police Station).

Title: Police Seize Ammunition From Drug Suspect in Narathiwat
Location: House No. 147/8, Moo 2, Kalawo Nuea Subdistrict, Mueang Narathiwat District, Narathiwat
Description: Police seized 19 rounds of .22-calibre ammunition, along with 2,822 methamphetamine tablets and 26.63 grams of ketamine when they arrested Ileeyas, 24, at his home in Kalawo Nuea Subdistrict, Mueang Narathiwat District, Narathiwat on 7 June 2026. Officers charged him with distribution of Category 1 and Category 2 narcotics and unlicensed possession of ammunition before referring the case for prosecution.

Title: Man Shoots Mother and Daughter, Then Kills Himself With Homemade Shotgun in Saraburi
Location: Rental rooms, Soi 7, Setthasamphan Road, Nong Khae Subdistrict, Nong Khae District, Saraburi
Description:
A man fatally shot himself with a craft handgun after using the same weapon to wound a mother and her 15-year-old daughter at a rental-room complex in Nong Khae, Saraburi, on 8 June 2026. Police identified the shooter as Prayong, 60, a neighbor who lived in a rental room across from the victims. Responding to an 8:10 a.m. emergency call, officers found the wounded pair — named in sources as Bussabaporn, 45, and her 15-year-old daughter — after fellow tenants helped carry them roughly 70 metres to seek help; Bussabaporn was shot in the left arm and her daughter was struck by shrapnel in the chest. Both were treated at Nong Khae Hospital and survived.

Prayong barricaded himself inside his own room after the shooting. Officers cordoned off the building and attempted to negotiate his surrender for an extended period; when a single gunshot was heard from inside, officers forced entry and found Prayong dead from a self-inflicted wound to the face, the shotgun beside his body. Neighbors said Prayong had been giving the 15-year-old large sums of money for over a year and grew resentful after she began seeing boys closer to her own age; police believe jealousy motivated the shooting.
Actors: Criminal Individual (Prayong, 60 — perpetrator, deceased by suicide); Police (Nong Khae Police Station, led by Pol Lt Suradet Phodaeng and Pol Lt Col Phichai Chaiyakul).

Title: Police Seize Weapons From Craft Manufacturer and Online Arms Dealer in Bangkok
Location: Khehasromklao Road, Khlong Song Ton Nun Subdistrict, Lat Krabang District, Bangkok
Description:
Police seized two craft/converted firearms, around 55 rounds of assorted ammunition, and gun-manufacturing equipment when they arrested Plakorn Sutthiart, 40, an alleged online arms dealer and craft manufacturer, in Lat Krabang District, Bangkok on 8 June 2026. Metropolitan Police Investigation Division 4 arranged a controlled purchase after an informant identified Plakorn as a seller in a Facebook group — with nearly 10,000 members — used to trade illegal firearms. Officers arranged to buy a .380-calibre pistol for THB 14,000 and arrested him as he made the handover at a parcel-delivery lot in Minburi District, then expanded the search to his rented room in the Khlong Song Ton Nun area.

The room search turned up a black .380-calibre semi-automatic pistol and a .22-calibre rifle, both homemade, along with 4 rounds of .380 ammunition, 33 rounds of .22 ammunition, a quantity of mixed rifle-caliber ammunition (30-06, 5.56mm, 9mm Luger), three magazines, twelve barrels in various stages of production, and tools including a welder, angle grinder, drill, hacksaw, vises, and calipers. Plakorn admitted converting purchased blank-firing pistols to fire live ammunition and selling them online to supplement his income after losing his factory job.
Weapons: 1 converted .380-calibre pistol; 1 .22-calibre craft rifle; 4 rounds .380-calibre ammunition; 33 rounds .22-calibre ammunition; mixed rifle-calibre ammunition, 20 rounds (30-06, 5.56mm, 9mm Luger); 12 barrels in various stages of production.
Actors: Criminal Individual (craft manufacturer and online gun seller); Police (Metropolitan Police Bureau Investigation Division 4, led by Pol Col Santi Kornkasem; operation overseen by Pol Lt Gen Siam Boonsom and other Metropolitan Police Bureau leadership; Minburi Police Station, receiving custody).

Title: Police Seize Pistol Used in Jealousy Murder-Suicide in Nakhon Ratchasima
Location: Mittraphap Road, Maklua Kao Subdistrict, Sung Noen District, Nakhon Ratchasima
Description:
Police recovered a 9mm pistol from inside the truck of a man who shot and killed his ex-girlfriend, a gas-station employee and TikTok personality known as 'Khao Pon,' before shooting himself on Mittraphap Road in Sung Noen District, Nakhon Ratchasima on 8 June 2026. The shooter, identified as Suthat Fodsoongnoen, 42, killed Chulaporn Rangkasong, 25–26, the evening before near a petrol station in Dao Rueang Subdistrict, Mueang Saraburi District, Saraburi.

Witnesses said Suthat pulled his truck up behind Chulaporn's car, fired twice, and fled; she was struck once in the back, with the round exiting through her chest, and once in the back of the head, and was found dead in her nightclothes beside her car. Her young son, roughly two to three years old, was in the vehicle at the time but was unharmed. Police believe the shooting was motivated either by jealousy after she reconciled with her current boyfriend, or by resentment after she exposed unflattering information about him that cost him his job days earlier. Officers tracked him to his hometown in Sung Noen District the following day; as he attempted to flee in his pickup truck, he shot himself once inside the vehicle, causing it to crash into a roadside barrier. Officers found him dead in the driver's seat with the 9mm pistol and ammunition inside the truck.
Actors: Criminal Individual (Suthat Fodsoongnoen, 42 — perpetrator, deceased by suicide); Police (Mueang Saraburi Police Station; Forensic Police Region 3).

Title: Police Seize Pistol and Crystal Meth From Drug Courier in Bangkok
Location: Soi Ratburana 37, Ratburana Subdistrict, Ratburana District, Bangkok
Description:
Police seized one unregistered 9mm pistol and 15 rounds of ammunition, along with a large quantity of crystal methamphetamine, when they arrested Warasak Boonsom, 44, a drug-network courier, at a rental house on Soi Ratburana 37, Ratburana District, Bangkok on 10 June 2026. Police had tracked the suspect's vehicle to the area; when officers searched the house they found the pistol, ammunition, and a set of car keys, then searched the car and recovered 38 bags of crystal meth from the trunk.

Following further questioning, Warasak directed officers to a second rented property in Phra Samut Chedi District, Samut Prakan, which he said he used to grind and pack the drug into hollow metal rods for transport; the search there turned up additional bags of crystal meth, the rods themselves (731 of them), grinding equipment, and digital scales.
Weapons: 1 unregistered 9mm semi-automatic pistol; 15 rounds of 9mm ammunition.
Actors: Criminal Individual (Warasak Boonsom, 44, arrested); Police (Metropolitan Police Bureau Investigation Division 8).

Title: Police Seize Three Firearms From Drug-Trafficking Couple in Chanthaburi
Location: Moo 7, Tak Phrom Subdistrict, Khlung District, Chanthaburi
Description:
Police seized three firearms, 97 rounds of ammunition, roughly a kilogram of crystal meth, over 20,000 methamphetamine tablets, and e-cigarette manufacturing equipment, when they arrested a fugitive couple in Tak Phrom Subdistrict, Khlung District, Chanthaburi on 11 June 2026. Officers and district administration staff acted on a tip from neighbors who complained of frequent drug parties at the property; when officers arrived and identified themselves, the pair attempted to flee but were caught after a foot chase.

A search of the property turned up 1,038.2 grams of crystal meth, 20,186 methamphetamine tablets, over 1,000 e-cigarette pod components, three firearms, 97 rounds of ammunition, cash, and valuables worth more than THB 5 million. Records showed the couple had outstanding 2024 drug-trafficking warrants for allegedly running narcotics from northern Thailand to agents in the south, and had been renting the property for roughly a month to evade capture.
Actor Type:
Criminal Organization
Police
Other State Agency
Actors: Criminal Organization (Thanaphat, 34, and Chanyanuch, 33 — wanted drug traffickers); Police (Tok Phrom Police Station; Chanthaburi Provincial Police, led by Pol Maj Gen Phadungsak Raksasuk and Pol Col Akkhaphong Soontharawiphat); Other State Agency (Khlung District administration).

Title: Officers Find Handgun During Teen Altercation on Pattaya Beach

Description: Pattaya City special affairs officers found a handgun on the beach in front of Central Pattaya, Bang Lamung District, Chon Buri on 12 June 2026, while responding to a report of an altercation between two groups of rival teenagers during a foot patrol. Officers called in police to take over the scene and referred those involved for prosecution.
Actor Type:
Youth Gang
Other State Agency
Police
Actors: Youth Gang; Other State Agency (Pattaya City special affairs officers); Police (responding officers).

Title: Police Seize Pistol and Tramadol From Illegal Vape Dealer in Sa Kaeo
Location: House, Moo 10, Wang Somboon Subdistrict, Wang Somboon District, Sa Kaeo
Description:
Police seized one .32-calibre Colt pistol and 100 rounds of ammunition along with 50 boxes of Tramadol-F capsules and over 330 illegal e-cigarette items, when they raided the home of Adisorn 'Saifa' Engprasert, 28, in Wang Somboon Subdistrict, Wang Somboon District, Sa Kaeo on 12 June 2026. The operation took place following a tip that the location was being used to store and sell illegal e-cigarettes to youths.

The search recovered 88 Salt Hub-brand e-cigarette devices, 62 cartridge heads, and 180 bottles of refill liquid, plus the Tramadol capsules and the pistol and ammunition, the latter hidden in a bedroom. Adisorn confessed that all the items were his, that he had sourced the products online, and that he had been selling to local teenagers for more than a year.
Weapons: 1 .32-calibre Colt pistol; 50 rounds .32-calibre ammunition; 50 rounds .380-calibre ammunition.

Title: Police Seize Craft Rifle From Man Who Assaulted Woman With Hammer in Chiang Rai
Location: Moo 6, Pa Hung Subdistrict, Phan District, Chiang Rai
Description:
Police seized two craft rifles when they arrested a male suspect at his residence for seriously injuring a woman with a hammer in Pa Hung Subdistrict, Phan District, Chiang Rai on 13 June 2026. Officers received a report of an assault at a home in Moo 6 and traced the suspect with the assistance of the Pa Hung SAO headman; the victim was treated by emergency responders and taken to Phan Hospital.

A search of the suspect's home revealed a flint-lock craft rifle hidden in the kitchen. Under questioning, the suspect — Chon, 55 — confessed that he had previously sexually assaulted the victim on 7 June 2026 and had arranged to meet her on 13 June at the village headman's home to discuss compensation of THB 10,000; when negotiations failed, he struck her once with a craft air-rifle (breaking the barrel), then used a hammer to strike her several more times. Chon tested positive for methamphetamine.
Actors: Criminal Individual (Chon, 55, assailant and sex offender); Police (Phan Police Station, Chiang Rai).

Title: Ramkhamhaeng National Park Rangers Seize Unregistered Shotgun From Poacher in Sukhothai
Location: Khao Tra Nok Wua Hill, Moo 8, Muang Kao Subdistrict, Mueang District, Sukhothai
Description: Ramkhamhaeng National Park rangers seized one unregistered single-barrel shotgun and three rounds of shotgun ammunition from a poacher caught with a freshly killed wild boar sow inside the national park in Muang Kao Subdistrict, Mueang District, Sukhothai on 13 June 2026. Chief Wichai Tha-jaiwong led a patrol team to the Khao Tra Nok Wua Hill area, where rangers encountered and arrested the suspect on the spot, seizing the weapon, ammunition, a 41 kg female wild boar carcass, and a game bag. The suspect and evidence were transported to Muang Kao Police Station for prosecution under the National Park Act, Wildlife Conservation and Protection Act, and Firearms Act. Area Conservation Office 14 (Tak) Director Phanatchakorn Photibundit stated that Smart Patrol enforcement would be intensified in high-risk zones.
Weapons: 1 unregistered single-barrel shotgun (ปืนลูกซองเดี่ยวยาว, no registration number); 3 rounds of shotgun ammunition.
Actor Type:

Other State Agency
Police

Title: Police Seize Pistol for Murder Suspects in Songkhla
Location: Rubber plantation near Pho Wim Shrine, Moo 6, Padang Besar Subdistrict, Sadao District, Songkhla
Description: Police seized a registered 9mm Beretta pistol and 20 rounds of ammunition after arresting two suspects in connection with the premeditated shooting of Wayu (alias "Beer"), 36, in a rubber plantation in Padang Besar Subdistrict, Sadao District, Songkhla on 14 June 2026. Officers found Wayu kneeling face-down with his hands pressed together, shot four times; the body position suggested the victim had been made to kneel before being executed. The initial investigation indicated a personal grudge and mockery among bullfighting associates as the motive. On 16 June, police arrested two suspects: Wanai (alias "Bae"), 40, identified as the shooter, and Yusob (alias "Sob"), 39, identified as aider and abettor. Both confessed.
Weapons: 1 registered 9mm Beretta pistol; 20 rounds of 9mm ammunition.
Actors: Criminal Individual (Wanai alias "Bae", 40 — shooter; Yusob alias "Sob", 39 — aider and abettor; both arrested); Police (Padang Besar Police Station; Songkhla Provincial Police Region 9 task force, led by Pol Lt Col Bunsongwit Hongsaeng and Pol Col Khomthet Pao-in).

Title: Police Seize Pistol from Border Patrol Officer Who Shot Taxi Drivers in Bangkok
Location: Soi Pracha Songkhro 38, Din Daeng Subdistrict, Din Daeng District, Bangkok
Description: Police seized a 9mm SIG Sauer service pistol from Pol Pvt Namthap Phakwattana, 33, a Royal Thai Border Patrol Police officer on personal leave, after he shot and killed one motorcycle taxi driver and wounded two others near Soi Pracha Songkhro 38, Din Daeng District, Bangkok on 14 June 2026. CCTV footage showed Namthap being kicked to the ground following a fare dispute, after which he drew his service pistol and fired, striking Charin Khunaraksa, 48, in the right thigh and left hand; Phurit Mangthisan, 37, multiple times in the chest, side, and thigh — Phurit died at the scene; and Kiatisak Srirat, 46, in the thigh from a stray round. Daily News reported a second fatality among the wounded. Namthap fled by taxi but surrendered himself and his weapon to police and subsequently appeared before the Criminal Court, which approved bail at THB 500,000 with conditions. BPP Command suspended Namthap pending a disciplinary inquiry.
Actors: Police (Huai Khwang Police Station investigators; Makkasan Police Station, receiving surrender; BPP Command — disciplinary authority); Former Police (Pol Pvt Namthap Phakwattana, 33, BPP — suspended, perpetrator).
Tags: Crime, Interpersonal dispute, Corruption/Abuse of Office

Title: Police Seize Pistol After Murder-Suicide in Nakhon Sawan
Location: Convenience store, Ban Kaeng Subdistrict, Mueang District, Nakhon Sawan
Description: Police seized a 9mm pistol after a man shot and killed a female convenience store manager in the head before fatally shooting himself at a convenience store in Ban Kaeng Subdistrict, Mueang District, Nakhon Sawan on 14 June 2026. The shooter, Wasan, 45, had been infatuated with store manager Thanyaphat, 34, who had rejected his advances. CCTV footage showed Wasan entering the store, briefly speaking with Thanyaphat, shooting her in the head and shoulder, then turning the weapon on himself; Thanyaphat died of her wounds. Neighbors said Wasan had been drinking at a nearby restaurant before the incident.
Actors: Criminal Individual (Wasan, 45 — perpetrator, deceased by suicide); Police (Mueang Nakhon Sawan Police Station).

Title: Police Seize SIG Sauer Pistol From Man at Vehicle Checkpoint in Bangkok
Location: Marayatdee Intersection, Phet-uthai Road, Bang Kapi Subdistrict, Huai Khwang District, Bangkok
Description: Police seized one 9mm SIG Sauer semi-automatic pistol, one magazine, and 11 rounds of ammunition from Patiphan, 40, after stopping his vehicle at a crime-prevention checkpoint at Marayatdee Intersection, Huai Khwang District, Bangkok on 15 June 2026. The suspect's nervous behavior prompted officers to search a black bag on the rear seat, where the loaded weapon was found. Under questioning, Patiphan admitted purchasing the pistol illegally for THB 30,000 from a Thai man known as "Noi," and that he held no firearms license.
Weapons: 1 9mm SIG Sauer semi-automatic pistol; 11 rounds of 9mm ammunition.
Actors: Criminal Individual (Patiphan, 40 — unlicensed firearms carrier, arrested); Police (Makkasan Police Station, led by Pol Col Oramphon Khundetchsamrit, Pol Lt Col Pitinan Kaewduangthian, Pol Lt Col Prasert Wongcharoen).

Title: Traffic Police Seize Craft Handgun From Suspect at Traffic Checkpoint in Bangkok
Location: Nut Nuea Road (On Nut Road), Prawet District, Bangkok
Description: Traffic police seized a craft handgun and a bladed weapon from a suspect stopped at a checkpoint on On Nut Road, Prawet District, Bangkok on 15 June 2026. The suspect was arrested and referred to investigators.
Actors: Police (Prawet Police Station traffic unit).

Title: Police Seize NORINCO Pistol and Yaba From Drug Suspect in Chonburi
Location: CJ Convenience Store car park, Soi Allied, Moo 6, Bowin Subdistrict, Sriracha District, Chonburi
Description: Police seized one 9mm NORINCO semi-automatic pistol, 13 rounds of ammunition, 490 methamphetamine tablets, and 1.376 grams of crystal meth from Thoetphan, 47, in the car park of a CJ convenience store in Bowin Subdistrict, Sriracha District, Chon Buri on 16 June 2026. Officers stopped Thoetphan in the car park and found all contraband on his person after a search.
Weapons: 1 9mm NORINCO semi-automatic pistol; 13 rounds of 9mm ammunition.
Actors: Criminal Individual (Thoetphan, 47, from Chon Buri Province, arrested); Police (Bowin Police Station, Sriracha, led by Pol Col Krit Thitathan and Pol Lt Col Somsak Butta).

Title: Police Seize Shotgun and Pistol After Two Killed During Domestic Dispute in Nakhon Si Thammarat
Location: Private residence, Mueang District, Nakhon Si Thammarat
Description: Police seized one shotgun and one 9mm pistol at a private residence after a double shooting left a male district official and a female cooperative office employee dead in Mueang District, Nakhon Si Thammarat on 16 June 2026. Neighbors and relatives who heard a loud argument followed by gunshots entered the residence and found both individuals dead. The male victim — a contract employee of Mueang Nakhon Si Thammarat District Office — had been struck by a 9mm round; the female victim — a staff member of the Nakhon Si Thammarat Public Health Cooperative — had been struck by a shotgun round. Officers found a shotgun on the bed and a short-barrel pistol near the male victim's feet, along with one expended shotgun casing and one expended 9mm casing. Investigators attributed the killings to accumulated jealousy and conflict between the couple, who had cohabited for some time.
Actors: Police (Mueang Nakhon Si Thammarat Police Station).

Title: Police Seize Converted Pistol from Suspects in Rap Feud in Udon Thani
Location: Mueang District, Udon Thani
Description: Police seized one converted blank-firing pistol when they arrested rapper Thijet "Young J" Robert, 22, and two associates on 16 June 2026 after they carried out a drive-by shooting targeting a former friend in Soi Nam Khem, Mueang Udon Thani on 13 June 2026. On 13 June, Thanakorn (alias "Om"), 22, reported to police that the three approached his rented accommodation; one associate, aged 16, fired one shot before the firearm jammed and the group fled. Young J had posted a video of the incident on social media. Investigators arrested all three at a hotel in the city on 16 June. Young J admitted the shooting, attributing the motive to a romantic dispute involving Om's former girlfriend, and stated he had purchased the converted blank-firing pistol for THB 22,000. Young J also confessed to taking multiple narcotics prior to composing music, stating he believed rappers who went to prison became more famous; the station commander publicly refuted this view.
Actors: Criminal Individual (Thijet "Young J" Robert alias "TJ Robert", 22 — organiser, arrested; Nattawut Namwiset, 22 — associate, arrested; Phopai, pseudonym, 16 — shooter, arrested); Police (Mueang Udon Thani Police Station investigation team, led by Pol Col Phatthana-wong Chanpol, Pol Lt Col Phichet Pakkhetthati, Pol Lt Col Phakhaphon Phonphak, and Pol Sgt Maj Banthoen Thapyotha).

Title: Border Patrol Seize Pistol and Methamphetamine Tablets After Armed Clash in Chiang Mai
Location: Public road, Ban Pong Hai, Moo 15, Mae Sao Subdistrict, Mae Ai District, Chiang Mai
Description: Border Patrol Police seized one 11mm pistol and approximately 2.8 million methamphetamine tablets from a suspect vehicle after a firefight with a transnational drug-trafficking convoy in Mae Sao Subdistrict, Mae Ai District, Chiang Mai on 17 June 2026. One member of the drug-trafficking group was found dead approximately 100 meters from the scene; the remaining members escaped. Personnel had deployed to intercept a suspected shipment when they observed a suspicious Chiang Rai-registered vehicle enter the Ban Pong Hai public road; when signaled to stop, the occupants fled and immediately opened fire, triggering a firefight lasting over ten minutes. Officers subsequently found approximately 14 improvised sacks inside the vehicle containing the methamphetamine tablets, plus the pistol and two mobile phones.
Actors: Criminal Organization (transnational drug-trafficking network; one member killed, others at large); Police (Border Patrol Police Region 33 / BPP Company 334, led by Pol Col Phaduengkiat Phantaranontaka, Pol Lt Col Thawatchai Surintha, Pol Lt Col Ananwat Rattanawichai).

Title: Police Seize MAT-49 Submachine Gun from Drug Traffickers Following Dispute in Nonthaburi
Location: Phra Nang Klao area, Bang Krasor Subdistrict, Mueang District, Nonthaburi
Description: Police seized one 9mm MAT-49 submachine gun and drugs from the luxury condo of a wanted suspect in Bang Krasor Subdistrict, Mueang District, Nonthaburi on 17–18 June 2026; the suspect was arrested in a follow-up operation on 21 June. Phiraphat, 31, arrived at the condo to collect a drug debt of THB 300,000 from Thep, 31, a former fellow inmate; Thep assaulted him with the submachine gun and fired one warning shot inside the room. Phiraphat wrestled the firearm away, fled down the emergency stairwell, and arrived at Phra Nang Klao Hospital reportedly armed with a separate 9mm pistol purchased for THB 20,000. Thep then fled with his wife and seven-month-old child in a white BMW. On 18 June, detectives searched the room and found bloodstains, the MAT-49 submachine gun, 54 grams of crystal meth, 70 grams of ketamine, 37 bags of GHB-type liquid, and 860 MDMA tablets, along with digital scales and packing equipment. On 21 June, officers arrested Thep and his wife at a car wash in Bang Lamung, Chon Buri, where they were found swapping license plates.
Actors: Criminal Organization (drug trafficking network; Thep, 31 — suspect, arrested; Thep's wife, 21 — arrested); Police (Rattanathibet Police Station; Nonthaburi Provincial Police; Region 1 Investigation Police; Nonthaburi Provincial Investigation).

Title: Law Enforcement Seizes Pistol From Drug User Threatening Family in Bangkok
Location: Soi Nakkila Laeam Thong 5, Thap Chang Subdistrict, Saphan Sung District, Bangkok
Description: ONCB Bangkok officers seized one pistol and approximately eight rounds of ammunition from Thanaphat after responding to an emergency hotline alert about a drug-affected individual threatening family members with a knife in Thap Chang Subdistrict, Saphan Sung District, Bangkok on 17 June 2026. Officers found Thanaphat severely intoxicated, exhibiting paranoid behavior, having threatened and beaten his younger sister. Officers restrained him and, searching his bedroom, found a semi-automatic pistol on the bed. Thanaphat was taken to Sirindhorn Hospital for a urine test, which confirmed the presence of narcotics.
Actor Type:

Other State Agency

Title: Police Seize Three Pistols and Over THB 10 Million in Assets From Drug Kingpin in Ratchaburi
Location: Moo 1, Ban Kha Subdistrict; Moo 3 and Moo 6, Ban Bueng Subdistrict, Ban Kha District, Ratchaburi
Description: Police seized three pistols and 149 rounds of ammunition when they arrested Sawrawut Kham-charoen (alias "Golf Ban Bueng"), a major drug trafficker, at his home in Ban Kha District, Ratchaburi on 17 June 2026, simultaneously searching three properties. Officers found two 9mm pistols, one .45-calibre pistol, 48 rounds of .45 ammunition, and 101 rounds of 9mm ammunition across the properties, along with vehicles, financial assets, and a mobile phone containing extensive drug distribution contact records and customer lists. Sawrawut, who claimed to be a lemongrass and galangal farmer, had been under surveillance for an extended period after multiple arrested lower-level dealers identified him as a major supplier. All seized assets were referred to the ONCB for asset-tracing and financial investigation.
Weapons: 2 9mm pistols; 1 .45-calibre pistol; 48 rounds of .45 ammunition; 101 rounds of 9mm ammunition.
Actor Type:
Criminal Organization
Police
Other State Agency

Title: Police Seize Three Registered Firearms From Cable Theft Gang in Bangkok
Location: Lat Krabang District, Bangkok
Description: Police detectives seized three registered firearms and 51 rounds of ammunition from two members of a commercial cable theft gang after arresting them in Lat Krabang District, Bangkok on 17 June 2026. The investigation originated from a 9 May report that unknown persons had stolen approximately 570 meters of electrical cable from a shopping mall on Ramkhamhaeng Road, causing nearly THB 1 million in damages; the cable was traced to a scrap dealer in Samut Prakan where it had been sold for THB 970,000. Detectives arrested Nai Surasak Ket-kaeo, 49, at a rental room, then located Nai Phradorn, 39, driving a Porsche near his Lat Krabang residence, where a search yielded: a .380-calibre Colt semi-automatic pistol (reg. GT.5705728) with 12 rounds; a 9mm Glock 43X semi-automatic pistol (reg. GT.6405716) with 14 rounds; and a 12-gauge Remington 870 pump shotgun (reg. GT.5462481) with 25 rounds — all registered in the name of Phradorn's wife. A third suspect, "Keng," was believed to have fled.
Weapons: 1 .380-calibre Colt semi-automatic pistol (reg. GT.5705728); 1 9mm Glock 43X pistol (reg. GT.6405716); 1 12-gauge Remington 870 shotgun (reg. GT.5462481); 12 rounds of .380 ammunition; 14 rounds of 9mm ammunition; 25 rounds of 12-gauge ammunition.
Actors: Criminal Organization (professional cable theft crew); Police (Hua Mak Police Station, led by Pol Col Prasopchok Eammphinit, Pol Lt Col Nattawat Tharak, Pol Lt Col Bamphen Namchwi).

Title: Police Seize Converted .380 Pistol From Teenager in Bangkok
Location: Soi Charoen Phatthana 4, Bang Chan Subdistrict, Khlong Sam Wa District, Bangkok
Description: Police seized one converted .380-calibre pistol and five rounds of .380 ammunition from an 18-year-old male suspect displaying suspicious behavior on a motorcycle in Soi Charoen Phatthana 4, Bang Chan Subdistrict, Khlong Sam Wa District, Bangkok on 19 June 2026. Officers on a late-night patrol identified themselves, conducted a lawful search of his backpack, and recovered the items. The suspect was arrested.
Actors: Criminal Individual (18-year-old male suspect, arrested); Police (Minburi Police Station, led by Pol Col Somphot Thongmun).

Title: Park Rangers Seize Craft Firearms and Ammunition From Poachers in Phetchaburi
Location: Moo 6, Pa Deng Subdistrict, Kaeng Krachan District, Phetchaburi
Description: Kaeng Krachan National Park rangers seized a craft flint-lock rifle, a craft gas pistol, and approximately six rounds of ammunition from two poaching suspects inside the UNESCO World Heritage park in Pa Deng Subdistrict, Kaeng Krachan District, Phetchaburi on 18–19 June 2026. During a four-day patrol (17–20 June), rangers on 18 June ambushed two suspicious individuals after a ten-minute wait, arresting one — Pubo, a Karen hill-tribe resident — who was found with the carcass of a mature male serow showing a .22mm gunshot wound and a bladed wound. Pubo stated his uncle had fired the shot. On 19 June, a follow-up search of the home of Jobu yielded the craft weapons and ammunition, along with wildlife traps and wildlife products including four serow horns, four serow bile sacs, three bottles of serow oil, porcupine quills, six wild boar tusks, two bear fangs, and eight small tiger fangs.
Weapons: 1 craft flint-lock rifle; 1 craft gas pistol; ~6 rounds of ammunition.
Actor Type:
Criminal Individual
Other State Agency
Military
Police
