Title: Police Seize Craft Handgun After Deadly Accidental Discharge in Ubon Ratchathani
Location: Ban Nong Hai, Moo 2, Non Klang Subdistrict, Phibun Mangsahan District, Ubon Ratchathani
Description: Police seized one craft handgun from Thinkon, 37, after he shot and killed his younger friend Sarawut, 20, while drunkenly handling the weapon at a residence in Non Klang Subdistrict, Phibun Mangsahan District, Ubon Ratchathani on 20 June 2026. Thinkon and Sarawut had been eating and drinking together, and Thinkon became heavily intoxicated. He produced the homemade firearm and began playing with it, whereupon it discharged and struck Sarawut in the right eye socket, killing him at the scene. Thinkon regained his composure, called police, and surrendered himself along with the weapon. Under questioning, Thinkon claimed the discharge was accidental.
Actors: Police (Phibun Mangsahan Police Station, led by Pol Col Supoj Jongutsa, Pol Lt Col Thawatchai Samrantsuk).

Title: Lion Dance Troupe Leader Shoots Neighbour Dead, Wounds Two More in Bangkok
Location: Trok Saphan Yao Soi 7, Soi Somdet Phra Chao Taksin 42, Dao Khanong Subdistrict, Thonburi District, Bangkok
Description: Police seized a firearm and ammunition from a man known as "Lek," aged approximately 55–58, a lion dance troupe leader, after he fatally shot a neighbor and wounded two others near his home in Dao Khanong Subdistrict, Thonburi District, Bangkok on 20 June 2026. Lek had returned home in a heavily intoxicated state and began discharging his firearm into the air multiple times, disturbing residents. When Pornpha, 44, went to confront him, Lek turned the weapon on her and fired; a round struck her in the throat and she eventually succumbed to her wounds. Pornpha's partner and daughter attempted to disarm Lek. During the struggle Kitti sustained a broken arm and a graze to the face; Wannapha was struck in the right cheek by a round that passed through to the left cheek, also seriously injured. The victim's eldest daughter, Toei, 26, stated the families had had friction with Lek for over 20 years due to his habitual drunken disturbances, and that he had fired into the air on multiple occasions. The perpetrator was apprehended inside his home.
Actors: Criminal Individual ("Lek," approximately 55–58 years old, lion dance troupe leader — perpetrator, arrested); Police (Bukkhalo Police Station).

Title: Youth Gang Shoots 16-Year-Old Motorcyclist Dead on Road in Phatthalung
Location: Moo 10, Khok Sak Subdistrict, Bang Kaeo District, Phatthalung
Description: Police searched for two firearms — reportedly dumped in an abandoned mine pit — when they arrested four suspects aged 16–24 who shot and killed Thiraphon Chungnoen (alias "Aof"), 16, a motorcyclist, in Takhomot District, Phatthalung on 21 June 2026. Aof was driving to watch a car modification gathering when a pickup truck carrying the suspects cut in front of him; a road confrontation ensued. One of the occupants fired a single shot that struck Aof in the throat, causing him to crash; he died of the combined injuries. On 21 June, detectives identified and arrested the suspects, including Anwa (alias), 18, and Krawich Kaewratthana (alias "Beer"), 16, identified as the shooters, and Yutthasan Phetkong, 24, and Piyawat Benuwong, 19. Anwa led officers to an abandoned mine pit in Moo 10, Khok Sak Subdistrict, Bang Kaeo District, where the weapons had been thrown. Dive teams searched for over an hour but were unable to recover the firearms due to depth and poor visibility.
Actors: Youth Gang (Anwa, 18 — suspect shooter; Krawich "Beer," 16 — suspect shooter; Yutthasan Phetkong, 24 — driver/passenger; Piyawat Benuwong, 19 — driver/passenger); Police (Takhomot Police Station).

Title: Police Seize Firearms From Loan Shark Brothers in Nakhon Si Thammarat
Location: Moo 5, Sichon Subdistrict, Sichon District, Nakhon Si Thammarat
Description: Cyber police seized seven firearms and 72 rounds of ammunition from two brothers who ran an illegal loan-sharking operation and habitually posted images of their arsenal on social media to intimidate debtors, in Sichon District, Nakhon Si Thammarat on 21 June 2026. Officers raided two locations in Sichon Subdistrict. At the first location, Chatchai alias "Yi Lok Hlek," 25, was arrested. Officers seized six firearms from the premises: one 9mm CZ semi-automatic pistol, one 9mm Smith & Wesson semi-automatic pistol, one .22-calibre craft pistol (with 3 rounds), one .45-calibre (11mm) Remington semi-automatic pistol (with 8 rounds in the magazine), one 9mm Glock semi-automatic pistol (with 15 rounds and 43 Bullet Master brand rounds), and one .357-calibre pen gun (with 3 rounds), along with a knuckle-duster and various knives. Follow-up searching — prompted by data on Chatchai's seized mobile phone — led to a rented room in Khun Talae Subdistrict, Suratthani Province, where a seventh firearm, a craft pistol, was found. Chatchai confessed to running an informal loan scheme at 10% daily interest (floating rate) targeting cockfighting enthusiasts and local residents, and claimed the firearms were a personal collection. At the second location, Chatchai's brother Chakrit alias "Ton Lok Hlek," 26, was arrested. No firearms were found at this address, but officers seized eight motorcycles, two sedans, one pickup truck, gold necklaces, gold Buddhas, and gold bars — assets accepted as collateral for informal loans at 5% daily interest over approximately two years.
Weapons: 1 9mm CZ pistol; 1 9mm Smith & Wesson pistol; 1 .22-calibre craft pistol; 1 .45-calibre Remington pistol; 1 9mm Glock pistol; 1 .357-calibre pen gun; 1 craft pistol; 3 rounds of .22 caliber ammunition; 8 rounds of .45 caliber ammunition; 58 rounds of 9mm ammunition; 3 rounds of .357 caliber ammunition.
Actors: Criminal Organization (Chatchai alias "Yi Lok Hlek," 25, and Chakrit alias "Ton Lok Hlek," 26 — brothers, loan sharks, arrested); Police (TCSD Sub-division 5 / Technology Crime Suppression Bureau Region 5, led by Pol Maj Gen Suraphon Prembutr, Pol Brig Gen Sila Kanchanak, Pol Col Sutthichai Thinpho).

Title: Police Seize Converted Pistol in Deadly Confrontation With Drug Trafficker in Ratchaburi
Location: House no. 23, Moo 2, Chamrae Subdistrict, Photharam District, Ratchaburi
Description: Police seized one converted 9mm pistol from Kittiphum Yaemklat alias "Phum Chamrae," 38, a drug trafficking suspect who opened fire on police volunteers and was killed in an exchange of fire during an attempted arrest at his mother's home in Chamrae Subdistrict, Photharam District, Ratchaburi on 22 June 2026. Officers tracked Kittiphum from his residence in Ban Pong District after learning he had traveled to his mother's home. Upon arrival, they found his vehicle parked outside and approached to conduct a search, at which point Kittiphum — seated inside the vehicle — opened fire without warning. A police volunteer, Thanakorn Phaengphon, 45, was struck and seriously wounded. Responding officers returned fire. Police cordoned off the vehicle and attempted to negotiate surrender for over one hour. Receiving no response, officers used ballistic shields and a hammer to break into the vehicle. Kittiphum was found dead in the driver's seat, his right hand still holding a 9mm pistol on his chest, with multiple gunshot wounds to his right arm and torso. Three pistol magazines and one imitation firearm were also found inside the vehicle, along with methamphetamine tablets, crystal methamphetamine, and a quantity of cash.
Actors: Criminal Individual (Kittiphum Yaemklat alias "Phum Chamrae," 38 — drug trafficking suspect); Police (Ban Pong Police Station, led by Pol Col Kobchok Lektrakun; Photharam Police Station; Ratchaburi Provincial Police; Region 7 Police Investigation Unit "Indri 7"; Ratchaburi Special Operations Unit).

Title: Police Seize Shotgun and Drugs From Suspects Stopped on Highway 106 in Lamphun
Location: Highway 106, Li District, Lamphun
Description: Police seized one unlicensed shotgun and nine rounds of 12-gauge ammunition along with methamphetamine tablets and crystal methamphetamine from two men travelling in a vehicle displaying suspicious behavior on Highway 106 in Li District, Lamphun on 22 June 2026. Officers from Lampang Highway Police spotted the vehicle and signaled it to stop. A search of the interior revealed 919 methamphetamine tablets, 12.96 grams of crystal methamphetamine, and the firearm. Both occupants tested positive for methamphetamine use.
Actors: Criminal Individual (drug suspects); Police (Lampang Highway Police).

Title: Police Seize Craft Pistol From Drug Suspect in Bangkok
Location: Soi Bang Khae 7, Yaek 12, Phetkasem Road, Bangkok
Description: Police seized one converted pistol and six rounds of .22 ammunition from Suthat alias "Dam Rong Than," 36, after stopping his vehicle at Soi Bang Khae 7, Yaek 12, Phetkasem Road, Bangkok on 22 June 2026. A tipoff had indicated Suthat was involved in drugs and habitually carried a firearm. When officers approached his vehicle he displayed suspicious behavior; a search of the driver's seat found the handgun and magazine concealed beneath it. Suthat claimed the firearm belonged to his wife's former husband.
Actors: Criminal Individual (Suthat alias "Dam Rong Than," 36 — drug suspect, arrested); Police (Phetkasem Police Station, led by Pol Col Pramot Chanbuenkeo).

Title: Police Recover Revolver Forgotten by Security Guard in Shopping Mall Restroom in Bangkok
Location: Seacon Square shopping mall (2nd floor), Sri Nakharin Road, Nong Bon Subdistrict, Prawet District, Bangkok
Description: Police recovered a registered revolver left unattended by a security guard in a public restroom at Seacon Square shopping mall in Prawet District, Bangkok on 22 June 2026. Police were alerted that mall patrons found a firearm in a restroom. Officers established that the weapon was a registered firearm and the owner was a legitimate security guard operating within the mall. The guard stated that he had entered the restroom while rushing to complete a cash transfer at an in-mall bank, removed the pistol for convenience, and left the premises without it. Police administered a formal caution for negligence and returned the firearm to its owner.
Actors: Unknown/Unaffiliated Entity (security guard); Police (Prawet Metropolitan Police Station).

Title: Police Seize Revolver After Deadly Family Dispute in Phatthalung
Location: Koh Mak Subdistrict, Pak Phayun District, Phatthalung
Description: Police recovered one .32-calibre short pistol and two rounds from Somchok (surname withheld), 62, who surrendered himself after shooting and killing his son-in-law Rungsarn, 31, in Koh Mak Subdistrict, Pak Phayun District, Phatthalung on 22 June 2026. Rungsarn, a shrimp paste trader, was preparing to deliver goods to Nakhon Si Thammarat when an unidentified assailant approached on foot and shot him at point-blank range before fleeing without a vehicle. Investigators identified Somchok — the victim's father-in-law — as the primary suspect and visited his home. Somchok surrendered the pistol and confessed that the shooting arose from prolonged family conflict with his son-in-law: on the night of the incident, the victim had argued violently with his wife (Somchok's daughter), threatened to stab her with a scraper, and then threatened to kill another of Somchok's children. Somchok said he acted out of rage.
Actors: Criminal Individual (Somchok, 62 — father-in-law, perpetrator, self-surrendered); Police (Ko Nang Kham Police Station, led by Pol Lt Thawiwat Mingthong, Pol Lt Somchit Khunthongphet).

Title: Police Seize Craft Handgun and 245 Rounds From Online Seller in Pathum Thani
Location: Moo 1, Rangsit Subdistrict, Thanyaburi District, Pathum Thani
Description: Police seized one craft handgun and 245 rounds of ammunition from Patima, 28, after raiding his home in Rangsit Subdistrict, Thanyaburi District, Pathum Thani on 23 June 2026. Investigators had been monitoring a private Facebook group and identified Patima habitually posting photographs of illegal firearms to attract buyers. Officers raided his residence and seized one craft handgun and 245 rounds of ammunition in multiple calibres (.380, .38, .32, 12- and 20-gauge shotgun shells). Under questioning, Patima confessed to running the illegal trade for over a year: he sourced firearms from associates at THB 5,000–6,000 each and resold them at THB 8,000–10,000 per unit. He also admitted to purchasing ammunition in bulk from shooting ranges and reselling it in the Facebook group at approximately THB 400–500 per box. Investigators announced plans to expand the inquiry to dismantle the broader network and trace the ammunition supply chain.
Actors: Criminal Individual (Patima, 28 — illegal firearms broker, arrested); Police (TCSD Sub-division 4 / Technology Crime Suppression Bureau Region 2, led by Pol Maj Gen Suraphon Prembutr, Pol Brig Gen Sarayut Chunnawal).

Title: Police Seize Two Firearms From Man in Pattaya Hotel Car Park
Location: Nong Prue Subdistrict, Bang Lamung District, Chonburi
Description: Police seized one .38-calibre Smith & Wesson revolver, one craft handgun, and seven rounds of ammunition from Adunwit, 31, in the car park of a hotel in Nong Prue Subdistrict, Bang Lamung District, Chonburi on 23 June 2026. A Pattaya police patrol unit spotted Adunwit behaving suspiciously in the hotel car park and conducted a search, recovering the revolver with four rounds of .38 ammunition and the craft shotgun with three 12-gauge shells.
Weapons: 1 .38-calibre Smith & Wesson revolver; 1 craft short-barrelled shotgun; 4 rounds of .38 caliber ammunition; 3 rounds of 12-gauge ammunition.
Actors: Police (Pattaya Metropolitan Police Station, led by Pol Col Anek Sra-thong-yu; Patrol Unit 3).

Title: Police Seize Two Firearms During Nationwide Data Trafficking Crackdown
Location: Multiple locations across 16 provinces (press conference at Central Investigation Bureau headquarters, Chom Phon Subdistrict, Chatuchak District, Bangkok)
Description: Police seized two firearms and 42 rounds of ammunition — among a wide range of digital and financial evidence — during Operation "Cut Down Scam 2," a nationwide crackdown on a personal data trafficking network, with a press conference held at the Central Investigation Bureau (CIB) in Bangkok on 23 June 2026. CIB officers, working alongside the Ministry of Digital Economy and Society (DE) and the Personal Data Protection Committee (PDPC), searched 22 locations in 16 provinces and arrested nine suspects. The investigation built on an earlier Operation Cut Down Scam in late 2025 that had netted six suspects and revealed 9 million stolen personal data records. The expanded operation identified the records had grown to 9,616,199 individuals — including 477 national ID card photos — and were being sorted into target categories ("doctors," "teachers," "wealthy") before sale to call-center fraud networks. Cross-referencing with the Thai Police Online crime-reporting system matched the data to 13,677 case IDs representing economic damage of over THB 2 billion. The firearms and 42 rounds were among multiple seized items including computers, phones, SIM cards, bank books, ATM cards, and THB 775,000 in cash.
Actor Type:
Criminal Organization

Title: Police Seize LIFECARD Pistol and Converted Guns From Online Gun Sellers in Bangkok
Location: Kheha Rom Kloa housing estate, Minburi District, Bangkok
Description: Police seized more than 20 items including one LIFECARD .22 WMR handgun, one craft handgun, and at least 15 blank-firing guns awaiting conversion from three young men, aged 21–23, operating an illegal online firearms business in Kheha Rom Kloa, Minburi District, Bangkok on 24 June 2026. When officers surrounded the premises, two of the three suspects panicked and fled across a roof which collapsed under their weight, causing injuries. In addition to the firearms, police seized evidence linking the group to multiple ghost Facebook pages — including "Witthawat Jadhai," "King Pho Dam," "Mongkol Puentho," and "Ram Ruay Ngoen Thong Wi Song" — used to defraud customers by accepting payments and then blocking them without delivering goods. Suspects were transferred to Ko Klom Rom Kloa Police Station.
Weapons: 1 LIFECARD .22 WMR pocket pistol; 1 craft firearm; multiple blank-firing guns (number unspecified, reportedly being prepared for conversion); additional items totalling over 20 seized items (exact breakdown not reported).
Actors: Criminal Organization (online gun sellers); Police (Metropolitan Police 191; Special Branch Suppression Unit; Ko Klom Rom Kloa Police Station).
Tags: Crime, Online, Conversion, Seizure, Illicit transfer

Title: Authorities Seize 20 Illegal Firearms in Province-Wide Drug Crackdown in Narathiwat
Location: Province-wide, Narathiwat; launched from Tak Bai District
Description: Authorities seized 20 illegal firearms as part of a month-long crackdown on drug trafficking and organized crime networks in Narathiwat Province concluding on 24 June 2026 (operation period: 26 May–24 June 2026). Over the 30-day period, 227 drug cases were filed, 233 suspects arrested, and 15 additional dealers identified through follow-up operations. Drug seizures included 676,288 methamphetamine tablets, 500 kilograms of crystal methamphetamine, 180 kilograms of cannabis, and 27 grams of heroin and other narcotics. The operation also framed the firearms and drug crackdowns as targeting the financial supply chain of the BRN (Barisan Revolusi Nasional) insurgent group, and announced a new border fence project to extend fencing along the full Narathiwat border with Malaysia. Images showed at least one craft rifle, one shotgun, five pistols, and one revolver.
Weapons: 1 craft rifle; 1 shotgun; 5 pistols; 1 revolver; 12 unspecified firearms.

Title: Man Shoots Two Neighbors During Land Survey Dispute in Phetchaburi
Location: Soi 5, Ban Rai Luang, Tha Mai Ruak Subdistrict, Thayang District, Phetchaburi
Description: Police arrested Thanat (surname withheld) after he shot two people with a 9mm firearm during a land boundary dispute in Tha Mai Ruak Subdistrict, Thayang District, Phetchaburi on 25 June 2026. Land survey officials had been invited by the parties to measure a contested boundary. After the survey reached its final point, a verbal altercation broke out between Thanat and the opposing parties. Thanat returned to his house, retrieved his firearm, and fired multiple rounds at the two, striking them. Relatives loaded the victims into a pickup and drove them to Mahachai Phetrat Hospital. Thanat also pointed the weapon at the land survey official, but was told the official had no stake in the dispute and backed off. Thanat was arrested at his home, adjacent to the scene. Police found 11 spent casings of 9mm calibre at the scene; the firearm was not immediately located, as Thanat claimed to have thrown it into a body of water, though police were skeptical. The victim's attorney stated the parties had an ongoing court case over a public right-of-way, and neighborhood sources noted Thanat had a prior charge of attempted murder still before the courts and a history of firing shots into the air.
Actors: Criminal Individual (Thanat, surname withheld — perpetrator, arrested); Police (Tha Mai Ruak Police Station, led by Pol Col Kamonlat Arunphak Mongkhon, Pol Lt Akkraphong Buntrakun).

Title: Authorities Seize 9mm Pistol and Crystal Meth From Drug Suspects on Koh Phangan
Location: Unnamed address, Moo 5, Koh Phangan Subdistrict, Koh Phangan District, Surat Thani
Description: Authorities seized one 9mm pistol and 20 grams of crystal methamphetamine from two men at a residence on Koh Phangan, Surat Thani Province on 26 June 2026. Following reports of drug use at the address, police searched the premises and found the suspects along with drug paraphernalia, the drugs, the firearm, and THB 11,800 in cash. Both suspects confessed that the drugs and weapon were theirs. Note: the sole source (MGR Online) refers to the firearms-owning suspect as "Chamroen" in the charges section but identifies him as "Ice" earlier in the article — likely a discrepancy between alias and legal name within the source itself; only one report was available for this incident.
